channel.title: The Stable Cyclist
channel.description: The Stable Cyclist is a place where we gather to talk about bikes (mostly MTB), mentality and Mental Health. Come with as the host JP, invites you in to conversations with people he’s met over the years. We won’t have stories about how to train or how to win a bike race here. Instead we’ll hear about what goes on in these athlete’s minds and sometimes we’ll even dive into the deep end of the pool and talk about mental health
channel.itunes:author: John Peter

item.title: Sarah Lange | Finding Balance in Endurance Sports & the LifeTime Grand Prix
item.itunes:title: Sarah Lange | Finding Balance in Endurance Sports & the LifeTime Grand Prix
item.description:
Sarah Lange has quietly become one of the most consistent athletes in the LifeTime Grand Prix, hovering around the top 10 overall the past two seasons.  But her path to professional cycling wasn’t linear.

In this conversation, Sarah and JP talk about her journey from collegiate running to discovering cycling through adventure and eventually gravel racing. Along the way, they dive into the challenge of balancing endurance sport and nutrition — especially after coming from a background where weight and performance were tightly linked.

They discuss imposter syndrome, navigating success, and how to build a healthy relationship with performance at the highest level. Sarah also reflects on her 5th place finish at Unbound in 2023 and what it means to grow in confidence while staying grounded. As always, they wrap things up with Random Fire.

https://silca.cc/discount/thestablecyclistpod

 or use the code is "StableSilca15"

 

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Brendan Johnston | Teenage Cancer, PTSD, LifeTime Grand Prix & Riding Across Australia
item.itunes:title: Brendan Johnston | Teenage Cancer, PTSD, LifeTime Grand Prix & Riding Across Australia
item.description:
Brendan Johnston is one of the most respected athletes in the LifeTime Grand Prix, but his story goes far deeper than race results. At just 17 years old, Brendan was diagnosed with testicular cancer — a moment that reshaped his life and left lasting psychological scars. In this episode, Brendan opens up about the PTSD that followed, the fear of recurrence, and the years he spent avoiding the emotional weight of that diagnosis while building a professional career in mountain biking and gravel racing.

We also dive into his Perth to Sydney ride across Australia — a massive endurance effort to raise awareness for men’s mental health while chasing an FKT. Brendan shares what it’s like balancing elite racing with fatherhood, living abroad while raising a daughter, and how he’s learning to speak openly about struggles many men keep buried. It’s a surprisingly light, honest conversation full of humor, depth, and perspective from one of the biggest names in the LifeTime Grand Prix.

https://silca.cc/discount/thestablecyclistpod

 or use the code is "StableSilca15"

item.title: Kerry Werner | Life After Pro Racing & Reinventing Purpose in Cycling
item.itunes:title: Kerry Werner | Life After Pro Racing & Reinventing Purpose in Cycling
item.description:
Kerry Werner returns to the Stable Cyclist Podcast to talk about life after professional racing. In his last appearance, Kerry opened up about navigating major life transitions — a conversation that resonated deeply with listeners. This time, we dive into what it actually looks like on the other side of a pro career: shifting identity, redefining purpose, and finding meaning beyond the start line.

Kerry now works inside the sport in a new way, creating media content around race day while collaborating with Russell Finsterwald and The Black Bibs. We discuss the strange tension of covering the front of the race instead of being in it, the creative process behind storytelling in cycling, and where Kerry now draws his inspiration. As always, we wrap with Random Fire.

 

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Zach Calton on Resetting His Career, Tough Seasons, and Returning to the Lifetime Grand Prix
item.itunes:title: Zach Calton on Resetting His Career, Tough Seasons, and Returning to the Lifetime Grand Prix
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, Lifetime Grand Prix rider Zach Calton joins JP for an honest conversation about stepping away from professional cycling—and what it took to find his way back. Zach reflects on leaving the pro ranks to work full time, the pull that eventually brought him back to racing full time, and the difficult stretch that followed. From a brutal Cape Epic to a tough Unbound, Zach walks through a season that didn’t go to plan and how learning to laugh through the lows became part of the process.

The conversation centers on the turning point in June of 2025, when Zach hit the reset button, changed how he was approaching the sport, and went on a tear through the rest of the season—ultimately earning his way back into the Lifetime Grand Prix. We talk about what actually changed, how perspective reshaped performance, and what it means to rebuild confidence after public setbacks. The episode wraps with one of the most entertaining Random Fire segments yet, as Zach’s analytical sense of humor and easygoing vibe shine through.

 

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Julien Gagné — Racing Calm, Changing Paths, and Finding Flow in Gravel
item.itunes:title: Julien Gagné — Racing Calm, Changing Paths, and Finding Flow in Gravel
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, French-Canadian pro gravel racer Julien Gagné joins the show to talk about his path through the sport — from XCO mountain biking, to pro road racing, and ultimately to finding his place in gravel. Julien reflects on the process of reinvention that comes with changing disciplines, learning how to stay grounded while chasing big results, and what it’s taken to carve out a sustainable career in one of the most competitive eras of gravel racing.

We also dig into Julien’s breakout wins at Gravel Locos and CORE4, his move from privateer life with a local Canadian shop to joining Lunchbox Racing, and how he balances a naturally calm personality with the need to flip the switch when it’s time to race all-out. Along the way, Julien opens up about creativity, art, and why having an outlet away from the bike has become essential to both his happiness and performance — before wrapping things up with a classic round of Random Fire.

 https://silca.cc/discount/thestablecyclistpod or use the code is "thestablecyclistpod"

We also dig into Julien’s breakout wins at Gravel Locos and CORE4, his move from privateer life with a local Canadian shop to joining Lunchbox Racing, and how he balances a naturally calm personality with the need to flip the switch when it’s time to race all-out. Along the way, Julien opens up about creativity, art, and why having an outlet away from the bike has become essential to both his happiness and performance — before wrapping things up with a classic round of Random Fire. https://silca.cc/discount/thestablecyclistpod or use the code is "thestablecyclistpod" If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Cécile Lejeune on Reinvention, Health, and Her Path to the Lifetime Grand Prix
item.itunes:title: Cécile Lejeune on Reinvention, Health, and Her Path to the Lifetime Grand Prix
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, Cécile Lejeune joins JP for an honest conversation about reinvention, resilience, and the long road to the Lifetime Grand Prix. Cécile reflects on her early years as an endurance runner, the college injury that forced her to step away from the sport, and how cycling slowly became a new outlet for competition and self-discovery. Rather than a straight line, her path into gravel racing was shaped by patience, curiosity, and a willingness to rebuild from the ground up.

The conversation goes deeper as Cécile opens up about athlete health, including disordered eating, RED-S, and the pressures that can arise when performance and identity become intertwined. We also explore her progression in gravel racing—from racing on a tight budget to earning a place with Trek Factory Racing for the 2026 season—and what that evolution has taught her about sustainability, confidence, and growth. As always, the episode wraps with Random Fire, offering a lighter glimpse into Cécile’s personality beyond the results.

item.title: Chase Wark on AFIB, Big Gravel Results, and Finding Perspective After a Challenging Year
item.itunes:title: Chase Wark on AFIB, Big Gravel Results, and Finding Perspective After a Challenging Year
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, JP sits down in person with professional gravel racer Chase Wark for a candid conversation about performance, health, and perspective. 

Fresh off major results at Gravel Worlds and Gravel Nationals, Chase opens up about developing AFIB during the summer and the surreal moment he found himself in the emergency room just days before hosting the Day Across Minnesota. Together, they talk candidly about racing at a high level, listening to your body, and navigating uncertainty when everything feels like it’s moving fast. Beyond racing and health, the conversation explores growth, perspective, and hope. Chase shares how Lunchbox Racing expanded from a two-person operation into a four-rider team alongside Drew Dillman, what the second half of last year taught him about balance, and how his mindset heading into this season feels fundamentally different. The episode wraps with some much-needed levity during Random Fire, including Chase’s all-time wild karaoke pick — a reminder that even in heavy seasons, joy still has a seat at the table.

https://silca.cc/discount/thestablecyclistpod or the code is "thestablecyclistpod"

item.title: Dylan Johnson on Depression and Recovery and The Season That Fell Apart
item.itunes:title: Dylan Johnson on Depression and Recovery and The Season That Fell Apart
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, Dylan Johnson returns for a deeply honest conversation about a season that unraveled in ways most people never saw. While Dylan’s 2025 was publicly defined by being hit by a truck and fracturing his tibial plateau — a story covered widely elsewhere — this conversation goes beyond what everyone saw. Dylan opens up about how the long recovery, stalled progress, and feeling like he had nothing to show for the year slowly gave way to depression, challenging his sense of identity both on and off the bike.

JP and Dylan unpack what it was like to sit with that reality, how depression can quietly build during injury recovery, and what ultimately helped Dylan reckon with it and move forward. The episode closes on a lighter note with a round of random-fire questions that show a different, more playful side of Dylan — a reminder that even in difficult seasons, humor and humanity still have a place.

item.title: Bicycle Crumbs aka Richard Pool | Why Relationships Matter More Than Talent
item.itunes:title: Bicycle Crumbs aka Richard Pool | Why Relationships Matter More Than Talent
item.description:
In the first episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast for 2026, I sit down with Richard Pool, better known to many in the cycling world as Bicycle Crumbs. Richard is widely respected for his work in cycling marketing, including his roles at Speedvagen Cycles and Silca, and for the way he has built a career rooted in genuine relationships. In this conversation, we explore why relationships often matter more than talent — not just in the cycling industry, but in life — and how showing up, staying curious, and investing in people can shape unexpected career paths.

Richard shares stories from his early roots in gravel cycling, his transition through different corners of the bike world, and the role that shared adventures play in a meaningful life. From racing events like Mid South to chasing ambitious personal challenges — including the idea of attempting a 24-hour distance record around a roundabout — this episode dives into the importance of always having one big, epic goal on the calendar. We also reflect on how our own paths crossed, how collaboration naturally followed, and how those relationships ultimately led us to working together at Silca, telling deeper stories within the cycling community.

https://silca.cc/discount/thestablecyclistpod  or the code is "thestablecyclistpod"

#thestablecyclistpodcast #BicycleCrumbs #RelationshipsMatter
item.itunes:summary: In the first episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast for 2026, I sit down with Richard Pool, better known to many in the cycling world as Bicycle Crumbs. Richard is widely respected for his work in cycling marketing, including his roles at Speedvagen Cycles and Silca, and for the way he has built a career rooted in genuine relationships. In this conversation, we explore why relationships often matter more than talent — not just in the cycling industry, but in life — and how showing up, staying curious, and investing in people can shape unexpected career paths. Richard shares stories from his early roots in gravel cycling, his transition through different corners of the bike world, and the role that shared adventures play in a meaningful life. From racing events like Mid South to chasing ambitious personal challenges — including the idea of attempting a 24-hour distance record around a roundabout — this episode dives into the importance of always having one big, epic goal on the calendar. We also reflect on how our own paths crossed, how collaboration naturally followed, and how those relationships ultimately led us to working together at Silca, telling deeper stories within the cycling community. https://silca.cc/discount/thestablecyclistpod or the code is "thestablecyclistpod" If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com The companies supporting JP and this channel: Maplelag Resort - https://www.maplelag.com/ Wark's Cycling Services - https://www.warkscyclingservices.com/ Ignition Coach Co. - https://www.ignitioncoachco.com/ Gulo Composites - https://gulocomposites.com/ Flow Formulas - https://flowformulas.com/discount/JOHNPETER15 Wahoo Fitness - https://amzn.to/48RUSqe Owl City Cycles - https://www.owlcitycycles.com The Black Bibs - https://amzn.to/3XRlMtm Dawn to Dusk - https://amzn.to/48zKi8p Some of the above are affiliate links, but all are personally vetted and recommended Mental Health Advocacy - Go4Graham - https://www.go4graham.org/ Resources: 988 Lifeline: https://988lifeline.org/ International Bipolar Foundation: https://ibpf.org/ National Association for Mental Illness: https://www.nami.org/ #thestablecyclistpodcast #BicycleCrumbs #RelationshipsMatter

item.title: Not Like This: Ben Frederick on Survival, TBI, and Finding His Way Back
item.itunes:title: Not Like This: Ben Frederick on Survival, TBI, and Finding His Way Back
item.description:
Nearly a decade ago, American cyclocross racer Ben Frederick suffered a devastating crash that changed everything. What began as a concussion spiraled into a traumatic brain injury (TBI), years of post-concussion syndrome, and a long, uncertain recovery that cost Ben his career, his identity, and nearly his life.

As his physical recovery stalled, Ben fell into deep depression and developed an eating disorder — clinging to the one thing he felt he could still control. At rock bottom, facing a life-or-death moment, Ben made a defining decision: not like this.

In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, Ben shares his full story — from injury and identity loss to rebuilding his life piece by piece. We talk about traumatic brain injury, mental health in endurance sports, disordered eating, and the difficult process of finding meaning beyond performance.

That journey ultimately led Ben back to the cyclocross start line, culminating this season with a powerful return to the UCI Cyclocross World Cup, finishing on the lead lap in Tábor.

This conversation goes far beyond racing. It’s a story about survival, resilience, and redefining success when everything you thought defined you is taken away.

Whether you’re an athlete, cyclist, or someone navigating recovery after trauma, this episode offers a rare and honest look at what it really takes to keep going.

 

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: 2025 Pod Awards feat: Dylan Johnson, Kerry Werner, The Holy Spirit of Gravel & More
item.itunes:title: 2025 Pod Awards feat: Dylan Johnson, Kerry Werner, The Holy Spirit of Gravel & More
item.description:
Welcome to the first-ever Stable Cyclist Podcast Awards, a full celebration of the best, wildest, and most unforgettable moments from an incredible year of conversations in the cycling world. In this episode, JP and co-host Aaron look back at the stories that shaped the show—everything from the most downloaded episode to the most impactful story, the worst interview, the most bleeped swears, and even the most off-the-rails moments that somehow still made it into the final cut. This special awards show also features a surprising story from JP that definitively lands him in the Slytherin House, plus plenty of laughs, throwbacks, and behind-the-scenes insights.

We revisit highlights from an all-star lineup including Dylan Johnson, The Holy Spirit of Gravel, Scott McGill, Nathan & Marc Spratt, Kait Boyle, Spencer Martin, Andrew Vontz, Kerry Werner, Brad Huff, Ian Boswell, Justin McQuerry, Bobby Wintle, Pete Stetina, Chase Wark, and Ted King. Whether you’re new to the show or have been following every episode, this is the perfect way to catch up on the top stories, biggest laughs, and most meaningful moments of the year. Buckle up—this episode is a rollercoaster.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: TREY SHEPARD & THE SPRATT BROTHERS REUNITE TO TALK PRO GRAVEL | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: TREY SHEPARD & THE SPRATT BROTHERS REUNITE TO TALK PRO GRAVEL | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
This week on The Stable Cyclist, JP welcomes Nathan and Marc Spratt, along with special guest and longtime friend of the Spratts, Trey Shepard, for one of the most entertaining episodes of the year. The Spratt brothers share their journey from growing up in the Indiana triathlon scene to carving out unexpected careers as rising pros in the American gravel world — all while working full-time as engineers in Utah. Trey helps bring out some incredible stories, launching the conversation into hilarious territory that includes AliExpress bikes at Leadville and the chaos that only childhood friends can unpack together.

The episode also digs deep into TNE, the Salt Lake City group ride that the Spratts have proclaimed the greatest and hardest group ride on the planet — a place where limits get tested, egos get flattened, and legends are made. With humor, honesty, and zero pretense, Nathan and Marc pull back the curtain on what it’s like to be two of gravel’s most underrated dark horses. If you’re into pro gravel racing, good storytelling, or just love hearing fast guys roast each other, this episode is a guaranteed win.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: INSIDE BADLANDS, TRAKA 560 & UNBOUND XL w/ Chris Mehlman | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: INSIDE BADLANDS, TRAKA 560 & UNBOUND XL w/ Chris Mehlman | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
Chris Mehlman joins The Stable Cyclist Podcast for a deep dive into the mental and physical demands of ultra-distance gravel racing. In the past year alone, Chris finished 3rd at Badlands, and earned top-10 results at Unbound XL and Traka 560 — three of the hardest ultra gravel events in the world.

We break down the mental side of going long, what it takes to stay consistent in multi-day races, and how he manages the emotional and psychological load of ultra-endurance.

Chris and I also get into the business side of pro cycling: – working with sponsors – navigating the industry – balancing content, racing, and results – what it takes to build long-term partnerships

We also talk about how the two major race series are shaping up for next year, and why Chris ultimately chose to step away from XCO racing to find his stride — and success — in ultra gravel.

If you’re into gravel racing, long-distance strategy, mindset, or building a career in endurance sports… this one is for you.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: DANNY AWANG ON FILMING THE RISE OF CAM JONES | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: DANNY AWANG ON FILMING THE RISE OF CAM JONES | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
This week, JP sits down with Danny Awang — the powerhouse filmmaker behind Scott Bikes, Cam Jones, and one of the creative voices capturing EF at the Tour de France Femmes. Danny shares what it was like to follow Cam Jones during his explosive rise throughout the Lifetime Grand Prix, documenting the highs, lows, and surprising human moments that never make it into the final cut. With a front-row seat to some of the biggest storylines in gravel and world tour racing, Danny talks about the art of staying invisible behind the camera while capturing some of cycling’s most emotional and iconic scenes.

JP and Danny also dig into the deeper story of Danny’s entry into filmmaking — how an early experience with an incredibly accepting cycling community changed his life and set him on the creative path he’s on today. Together, they explore the importance of belonging, the mental side of creative work, and the joy of crafting stories that move people. And of course, the episode ends with the classic round of rapid-fire questions. If you love cycling media, filmmaking, or stories of unexpected opportunity, this conversation delivers.

item.title: GAS STATION SHOWDOWN (Feat. Dylan Johnson, Ian Boswell, Ben Delaney and more) | The Bootleg Miles
item.itunes:title: GAS STATION SHOWDOWN (Feat. Dylan Johnson, Ian Boswell, Ben Delaney and more) | The Bootleg Miles
item.description:
For the final Bootleg Miles episode of the year, JP dives into the ongoing debate from The Stable Cyclist Podcast and brings in answers from some of the biggest names in the sport: Gravel Hall-of-Famers Selene Yeager and Amanda Sheek, American Crit legend Brad Huff, gravel pros Justin McQuerry, Chase Wark and Dylan Johnson, WorldTour veterans Ian Boswell and Pete Stetina, TrainerRoad’s Jonathan Lee, journalist Ben Delaney, former US MTB National Champs Carson Beckett and Gordon Wadsworth and even the Holy Spirit of Gravel.

Along the way, JP breaks down rural gas station culture, his favorite stops in Minnesota and North Dakota, the genius of the “redneck ice sock,” and the simple joy of a cold Coke or a counter-top treat that definitely wasn’t made in a factory.

We’ll also take a ride out to Olson’s Service in Sentinel Butte, ND — a full-service station straight out of Cars and Route 66 history — and talk about why these places matter more than we think.

This episode wraps up a full season of gravel adventures, filmmaking, racing, and podcasting. Thanks for coming along for the ride — and remember: you are loved.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: FlexFitbyJohn | From Bike Fitter to Internet Tire Testing Authority | John Karrasch | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: FlexFitbyJohn | From Bike Fitter to Internet Tire Testing Authority | John Karrasch | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
This week, JP sits down with John Karrasch, better known as FlexFitByJohn, whose gravel tire rolling resistance and speed tests have taken the cycling world by storm. What started as a behind-the-scenes bike fitting career in Birmingham, Alabama turned into a meteoric rise as John became the go-to resource for accurate, data-driven tire performance testing. In this episode, John talks about how his meticulous work with the Chung method built trust across the gravel community, eventually landing him on the Escape Collective podcast with Ronan McLaughlin and establishing him as one of the most respected voices in modern cycling tech.

JP and John go deeper into what it feels like to transition from relative obscurity to becoming a widely recognized figure in the sport. They talk about imposter syndrome, validation from Robert Chung himself, and how to stay grounded when your identity is shifting and your work suddenly reaches thousands of riders. This episode blends cycling science, personal growth, and the human side of going viral — a must-listen for anyone who loves gear, gravel, or a good story of unexpected success.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: HANNAH SHELL: RACING, RV LIFE and EMPOWERING WOMEN | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: HANNAH SHELL: RACING, RV LIFE and EMPOWERING WOMEN | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
This week, JP sits down with Hannah Shell, a top-10 finisher in the Gravel Earth Series and one of the most recognizable riders in the pro gravel peloton. Hannah shares her winding path into gravel racing, how she balances high-level competition with her work at Gravel Worlds, and what it’s really like to pursue the sport at the elite level. Her insight into the evolving landscape of gravel offers a refreshing and honest look at what it takes to excel while staying grounded.

The conversation goes deeper into identity and the experience of being a six-foot-tall woman in a peloton where confidence and presence matter. Hannah talks about the power of strong female role models, the importance of women supporting women, and how she and her husband navigate full-time RV life throughout the racing season. They wrap up with classic rapid-fire questions, making this episode equal parts insightful, personal, and fun — a must-listen for fans of the growing gravel community.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: JONATHAN LEE ON IDENTITY, FAITH, AND FINDING HIMSELF BEYOND THE BIKE | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: JONATHAN LEE ON IDENTITY, FAITH, AND FINDING HIMSELF BEYOND THE BIKE | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
This week, JP welcomes Jonathan Lee, the widely recognized host of the TrainerRoad Podcast and a beloved voice in the cycling world. JP and Jonathan share a powerful full-circle moment — back in 2021, Jonathan interviewed JP on the Successful Athletes Podcast about living with Bipolar Disorder and rebuilding life through low-volume training. That conversation became a genesis point for JP, planting the seed that eventually grew into The Stable Cyclist Podcast. Now, Jonathan steps into the guest seat to reflect on his own path to TrainerRoad and how solving problems — for athletes, coworkers, and himself — has guided his career.

The conversation goes far beyond watts and workouts. JP and Jonathan dive into Jonathan’s life outside the studio: his work within his church community, managing training alongside a demanding life, and the deeper question of where identity is truly found. This is one of the most honest, grounded conversations you’ll hear — a look at the human behind one of cycling’s most recognizable podcast voices, exploring purpose, balance, vulnerability, and faith in a world that often values performance above everything else.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

JP and Jonathan share a powerful full-circle moment — back in 2021, Jonathan interviewed JP on the Successful Athletes Podcast about living with Bipolar Disorder and rebuilding life through low-volume training. That conversation became a genesis point for JP, planting the seed that eventually grew into The Stable Cyclist Podcast. Now, Jonathan steps into the guest seat to reflect on his own path to TrainerRoad and how solving problems — for athletes, coworkers, and himself — has guided his career. The conversation goes far beyond watts and workouts. JP and Jonathan dive into Jonathan’s life outside the studio: his work within his church community, managing training alongside a demanding life, and the deeper question of where identity is truly found. This is one of the most honest, grounded conversations you’ll hear — a look at the human behind one of cycling’s most recognizable podcast voices, exploring purpose, balance, vulnerability, and faith in a world that often values performance above everything else. If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com The companies supporting JP and this channel: Maplelag Resort - https://www.maplelag.com/ Wark's Cycling Services - https://www.warkscyclingservices.com/ Gulo Composites - https://gulocomposites.com/ Flow Formulas - https://flowformulas.com/discount/JOHNPETER15 Wahoo Fitness - https://www.wahoofitness.com Dawn to Dusk - https://www.https://dawntodusk.bike/ Owl City Cycles - https://www.owlcitycycles.com The Black Bibs - https://www.theblackbibs.com Ignition Coach Co. - https://www.ignitioncoachco.com/ Mental Health Advocacy - Go4Graham - https://www.go4graham.org/ Resources: 988 Lifeline: https://988lifeline.org/ International Bipolar Foundation: https://ibpf.org/ National Association for Mental Illness: https://www.nami.org/

item.title: BRETT ROTHMEYER ON PHOTOGRAPHING KEEGAN SWENSON AND THE HTSQD | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: BRETT ROTHMEYER ON PHOTOGRAPHING KEEGAN SWENSON AND THE HTSQD | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
This week, JP sits down with Brett Rothmeyer, the creative eye behind the photos of Keegan Swenson and the Santa Cruz htSQD. As part of JP’s ongoing “Creators in Cycling” series, Brett shares his long journey into the world of professional photography — from discovering his voice behind the lens to capturing some of cycling’s most defining moments. They dig into what it takes to shoot massive events like Unbound, Gravel Worlds, and Leadville, the intense travel schedule that comes with the territory, and the balance between art, exhaustion, and passion.

Brett also recounts his front-row experience at the UCI Marathon MTB World Championships, where Keegan Swenson claimed the top step and Brett was there to document the moment that defined a season. This episode gives an intimate look at the often unseen creative grind that shapes the stories, images, and emotions of the sport we love.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: KAIT BOYLE | RESILIENCE, RECOVERY AND MAKING HISTORY AT MARJI GESICK | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: KAIT BOYLE | RESILIENCE, RECOVERY AND MAKING HISTORY AT MARJI GESICK | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
This week’s guest is Kait Boyle, one of the most respected names in ultra-endurance mountain biking. Kait’s journey is one of transformation — from her early days in outdoor education to becoming a professional MTB racer and 24-Hour World Champion. She shares what drew her to the world of endurance riding, how it shaped her view on challenge and growth, and what it means to live a life defined by resilience.

We then dive into the car accident that nearly ended her racing career — a moment that forced her to rebuild physically, mentally, and emotionally. Kait reflects on how that experience reshaped her understanding of purpose and community. Finally, we explore her incredible performance at the 2025 Marji Gesick, where she became the first of three women ever to finish under 12 hours, earning the coveted belt buckle alongside Syd Schultz, Kristy McBride, and Julie Momber. Together, they made history — and Kait takes us inside the teamwork, grit, and mindset that made it possible.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: MADDY NUTT | THE COURAGE TO CHASE YOUR DREAMS | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: MADDY NUTT | THE COURAGE TO CHASE YOUR DREAMS | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
This week’s guest is Maddy Nutt (@maddy_nutt) a British professional gravel racer who just represented Team Great Britain at the UCI Gravel World Championships. Joining me from London, Maddy reflects on her incredible 2024 season and the journey that led her from a traditional job to racing full-time across the globe.

We dive deep into identity on this one as we talk about the reflections caused last year by her post-COVID health struggles and the heart complications that came into her life. Maddy opens up about taking that leaps into a new world moving from a regular job into full time gravel racing.  We also discuss her love of baked goods, and how she finds balance between competition, creativity, and recovery.

 

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: ALEXIS CARTIER | BIKEPACKING THE ENTIRE LIFETIME GRAND PRIX | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: ALEXIS CARTIER | BIKEPACKING THE ENTIRE LIFETIME GRAND PRIX | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
This week on The Stable Cyclist Podcast, JP welcomes Alexis Cartier, the Canadian gravel racer redefining what it means to commit to a cause. A professional rider from Montreal and passionate advocate for climate action, Alexis has made headlines this year by choosing to ride his bike to every race in the LifeTime Grand Prix, rather than flying or driving. Now over 10,000 miles into this journey, he joins JP to reflect on the lessons, the challenges, and the incredible experiences from a summer spent fully powered by pedals.

With the final race of the season — Big Sugar Gravel — on the horizon and a 2,000-mile ride home still ahead, Alexis opens up about how this mission has reshaped his outlook on the sport and the environment. Together, JP and Alexis explore the intersection of cycling, sustainability, and storytelling, reminding listeners that real change starts with the choices we make every day.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: TILLY SHULL | THE ART AND EMOTION OF GRAVEL MEDIA | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: TILLY SHULL | THE ART AND EMOTION OF GRAVEL MEDIA | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
In this special episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, JP sits down with gravel cycling’s photography star, Tilly Shull. Known for her stunning imagery and storytelling from inside the LifeTime Grand Prix, Tilly shares what life is really like behind the lens at some of the biggest gravel events in the world. She and JP talk about the intensity and emotion of photographing in Stillwater, OK, as wildfires threatened MidSouth 2024, and how those moments shaped the way she approaches her work.

The two also dive into lighter topics — from their favorite “on the road” foods to the best playlists for working in the studio — as well as a few behind-the-scenes stories from epic rides like Chase Wark and Ted King’s Mega MidSouth FKT. This episode kicks off a new once-a-month series where JP introduces listeners to the creative minds behind the cameras in the cycling world — the storytellers who capture the soul of gravel.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.itunes:summary: In this special episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, JP sits down with gravel cycling’s photography star, Tilly Shull. Known for her stunning imagery and storytelling from inside the LifeTime Grand Prix, Tilly shares what life is really like behind the lens at some of the biggest gravel events in the world. She and JP talk about the intensity and emotion of photographing in Stillwater, OK, as wildfires threatened MidSouth 2024, and how those moments shaped the way she approaches her work. The two also dive into lighter topics — from their favorite “on the road” foods to the best playlists for working in the studio — as well as a few behind-the-scenes stories from epic rides like Chase Wark and Ted King’s Mega MidSouth FKT. This episode kicks off a new once-a-month series where JP introduces listeners to the creative minds behind the cameras in the cycling world — the storytellers who capture the soul of gravel. item.title: JUSTIN MCQUERRY | LIFE IN PRO GRAVEL WITH TYPE 1 DIABETES | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: JUSTIN MCQUERRY | LIFE IN PRO GRAVEL WITH TYPE 1 DIABETES | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, JP is joined by professional gravel racer Justin McQuerry. Justin shares his journey from chasing a spot in the WorldTour to finding his home in the thriving world of gravel racing. Along the way, he opens up about the highs and lows of pursuing a career in cycling, and how the sport has evolved to provide opportunities outside of traditional road racing.

A major part of Justin’s story is his life with Type 1 Diabetes, a condition he has managed since infancy. JP and Justin dive into the unique challenges of racing at the highest level while balancing blood sugar, nutrition, and performance demands that few outside the diabetic community fully understand. This is a raw, inspiring, and eye-opening conversation about resilience, adaptation, and carving your own path in cycling.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: HOLY SPIRIT OF GRAVEL l FROM ANONYMOUS VOICE TO CONTENT ICON | THE STABLE CYCLIST PODCAST
item.itunes:title: HOLY SPIRIT OF GRAVEL l FROM ANONYMOUS VOICE TO CONTENT ICON | THE STABLE CYCLIST PODCAST
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, JP is joined by the one and only Holy Spirit of Gravel — one of the most unique and recognizable voices in the gravel community. What began as a fun, anonymous account built on wit and cycling passion has quickly become a movement. The Spirit shares her story of how she fell in love with gravel, why she started the account, and the decision to step out from behind the screen and become a front-facing part of the community.

The conversation dives deep into the rapid evolution of gravel cycling, the role content creators play in shaping the sport’s culture, and how storytelling has fueled its explosive growth. From the quirks that make gravel so different from road cycling to the ways in which community and humor have strengthened its identity, this episode is a must-listen for anyone who loves the sport and the voices that make it thrive.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.itunes:title: Podcast Update
item.description: Hey everyone, don't panic, we'll all be back together for more stories soon enough. I am just in the middle of the beginning of the school year and the middle of the soccer season and my time is extremely finite, so here's a little update. Hopefully we are back on track next week, or maybe later.

item.title: The Bicycle Station’s Trey Shepard on Balance, Humility & Gravel Life
item.itunes:title: The Bicycle Station’s Trey Shepard on Balance, Humility & Gravel Life
item.description:
This week on The Stable Cyclist Podcast, JP sits down with Trey Shepard — one half of the duo behind the viral bike build channel, The Bicycle Station. Alongside Jesse Smith, Trey has helped launch the channel into one of the fastest-growing platforms in the gravel cycling scene.

JP and Trey dive into the “holy crap” moment at Unbound when it felt like nearly everyone on course recognized Trey — and what it means to balance that kind of attention with humility. From sudden fame to staying grounded, this conversation captures why Trey is one of the nicest and most genuine voices in cycling.

Whether you’re a fan of gravel, YouTube, or just good storytelling, this is one you won’t want to miss.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: INNOKENTY ZAVYALOV | MOVING ON AND FINDING IDENTITY | THE STABLE CYCLIST PODCAST
item.itunes:title: INNOKENTY ZAVYALOV | MOVING ON AND FINDING IDENTITY | THE STABLE CYCLIST PODCAST
item.description:
Innokenty Zavyalov is a former professional road and gravel racer from Minneapolis, MN. After a career on the road, Inno made the move to gravel in the post pandemic era. He found quick results and big success but along the way he felt lost and ultimately, last year walked away from the sport.

This is a tremendous conversation recorded in person in Grand Forks, ND ahead of the Nepomuk Narly Gravel Race. It dives deep on identity, balance and finding who you are.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Post-A-Race Blues: How I Avoid the Mental Crash | The Bootleg Miles
item.itunes:title: Post-A-Race Blues: How I Avoid the Mental Crash | The Bootleg Miles
item.description:
After six years chasing the MDH100, I crossed the finish line in tears — not because it was over, but because the dream was finally real. But here’s the truth: once the glow fades, the post-race crash can creep in fast. If you’re not careful, that fog can pull you into depression — and I’ve been there.

In this episode of Bootleg Miles, I share the daily, mid-term, and long-term strategies I use to avoid the post-A-race slump, keep moving forward, and stay connected with my community. These tips have helped me as a rider living with Bipolar Disorder — but I’ve also heard from countless athletes (including pros) who deal with the same struggle.

If you’ve ever wondered “what now?” after a big race, this one’s for you.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

#MaahDaahHey100 #BootlegMiles #MentalHealth #UltraEndurance #CyclingLife #MentalHealthAwareness #BikeLife #cyclingmotivation #PostRaceRecovery #CyclingCommunity
item.itunes:summary:
After six years chasing the MDH100, I crossed the finish line in tears — not because it was over, but because the dream was finally real. But here’s the truth: once the glow fades, the post-race crash can creep in fast. If you’re not careful, that fog can pull you into depression — and I’ve been there. In this episode of Bootleg Miles, I share the daily, mid-term, and long-term strategies I use to avoid the post-A-race slump, keep moving forward, and stay connected with my community. These tips have helped me as a rider living with Bipolar Disorder — but I’ve also heard from countless athletes (including pros) who deal with the same struggle. If you’ve ever wondered “what now?” after a big race, this one’s for you. item.title: Kathryn Taylor | Girls Gone Gravel Founder and Community Builder | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Kathryn Taylor | Girls Gone Gravel Founder and Community Builder | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
Kathryn Taylor is the visionary founder behind Girls Gone Gravel, a groundbreaking community that connects and empowers women cyclists around the world. In this episode, Kathryn shares the origin story of Girls Gone Gravel — from starting a simple Instagram account to leading a thriving movement with events, online groups, and thousands of members. She opens up about the challenges of scaling the brand and the moment she realized she needed a team to help manage the explosive growth.

JP and Kathryn also dive deep into the real barriers women face in the cycling world, both on and off the bike, and how meaningful support from men can help break them down. Whether you’re a woman seeking community or a man looking to better support the riders in your life, this conversation is full of inspiration, insight, and actionable takeaways.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: EMERGENCY POD | JP and DEREK WALLACE TALK ABOUT THE MAAH DAAH HEY 100 | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: EMERGENCY POD | JP and DEREK WALLACE TALK ABOUT THE MAAH DAAH HEY 100 | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
Director of Photography Aaron Haugen sits down with JP and Derek to talk about their race at the Maah Daah Hey 100 on August 2nd.  The trio talks about what went well, what their low points were and why the race is so hard.  

But they also talk a lot about the value of friends and community and how the race weekend and their experience together galvanized their friendships more than ever.  This is an episode you don't want to skip!

item.title: Brad Huff on Purpose, Life After Racing and Mental Health | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Brad Huff on Purpose, Life After Racing and Mental Health | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
🚨 Content Warning: This episode includes open discussions about mental health, emotional struggles, and contains strong language. Please listen with care.

Brad Huff is a decorated American cyclist known for his sprinting power, charisma, and relentless work ethic. With a career spanning teams like Jelly Belly and Rally Pro Cycling, Brad was a fan favorite in the domestic peloton and beyond. But in this episode, we go far beyond race results and podiums. Brad and JP have a raw, emotional conversation about the mental toll of chasing dreams at all costs—and how sometimes, the hardest thing to do is ask for help.

This episode dives deep into the mental health struggles many athletes face, the courage it takes to shift priorities, and the miracle of finding connection when vulnerability meets community. Brad shares powerful insights from his post-racing life, especially his work with Project Dudeman in Tulsa, OK—a grassroots movement using bikes to bring men together, build real community, and create space for honest mental health conversations.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Adam Saban Talks About Coaching JP "Back to the Start" | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Adam Saban Talks About Coaching JP "Back to the Start" | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
When I started working on the Back to the Start project back in 2023, Adam Saban and I crossed paths. He offered his services to me for free as a coach. His guidance and support have been really important in me getting to this starting line. So I wanted to talk about some of the things we did. How we moved very slowly at first but in a way that also pushed me. Enjoy this one.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Joe Goettl | Resilience, Racing, and Making Media that Matters | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Joe Goettl | Resilience, Racing, and Making Media that Matters | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
Joe Goettl is more than just a professional gravel racer—he’s a storyteller, a survivor of chronic pain, and one of the sport’s rising media talents. In this episode, JP sits down with Joe to explore his unique path from a promising young runner in Minnesota to the harsh realities of long-term injury and physical setbacks. After years of battling chronic pain, Joe found a breakthrough through the Pain Academy, and his story is a masterclass in perseverance and mindset.

But Joe’s journey doesn’t stop at healing—he’s now thriving on the gravel scene, racing at the elite level and creating compelling content that captures the spirit of the sport. Joe and JP also get into the behind-the-scenes life of a media-savvy racer, the mental game required to push through adversity, and finish things off with some fan-favorite rapid fire questions. Whether you're chasing fitness, storytelling, or your own comeback, this episode will resonate.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Matthew Fisher | All Bodies on Bikes DFW | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Matthew Fisher | All Bodies on Bikes DFW | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
Matthew Fisher is the leader of the Dallas-Fort Worth chapter of All Bodies on Bikes. In this episode, Matt and JP talk about the difficulty of entry into the bike world for non-traditional individuals, how to ask the right questions at a bike shop to find the bike for you and the unmet dream of racing The Mid South.

https://www.allbodiesonbikes.com/about-the-program-midsouth

All Bodies on Bikes + Mid South Gravel have teamed up to bring 12 non-traditional athletes to Mid South Gravel 2026 for the ride of their lives, focused on joyful movement and fun! If that's you, hit the link above to find out more information.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Spencer Martin on Beyond the Peloton, The Move, and Finding Cycling
item.itunes:title: Spencer Martin on Beyond the Peloton, The Move, and Finding Cycling
item.description:
Spencer Martin is widely considered one of the sharpest minds in modern cycling analysis. As the creator of the Beyond the Peloton newsletter and podcast, Spencer has built a loyal following for his data-driven, narrative-rich takes on the world’s biggest races. He’s also the producer behind The Move, the popular WEDU podcast hosted by Lance Armstrong. In this episode, JP and Spencer explore how a kid growing up in rural Kansas in the early 2000s found cycling and built a career around it—eventually working with some of the sport’s biggest names.

This conversation is more than just power numbers and podiums. JP and Spencer dive into some of the most unforgettable races from the past two decades, sharing insight and personal memories that highlight what makes the sport so magnetic. And in true Stable Cyclist fashion, they close the episode with a spirited detour into Harry Potter—proving that you can be both a high-level tactician and a true fan at heart. Whether you're a hardcore roadie or just love great storytelling, this episode delivers.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: List Making at Lutsen Mountain | The Bootleg Miles
item.itunes:title: List Making at Lutsen Mountain | The Bootleg Miles
item.description:
In this episode of the Bootleg Miles, JP goes to Lutsen, MN where he is about to film the Lutsen 99er for the next episode of Off the Front, his series about Chase Wark. But JP wants to share something much more personal. On this ride, he shares how listmaking transformed his life after being diagnosed with Bipolar Disorder in 2020.

JP outlines how he uses lists as his primary weapon again Depression and how it helps him move forward. What do you or the people around you use as a coping mechanism to fight? If you are feeling brave, drop it in the comments below.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Carson Beckett on Becoming National Champ | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Carson Beckett on Becoming National Champ | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
Learn more about Dirt Camp Racing: https://www.dirtcampracing.com/

 

Carson Beckett is the current Elite Marathon National Champion and is preparing to defend his title at Carvin’s Cove in Roanoke, Virginia. In this episode, Carson sits down with JP to unpack the journey to becoming a national champion—what it meant to chase the stars-and-stripes jersey, the deep personal affirmation that came with victory, and what the "view from the top" really looks like. It’s an honest look at performance, purpose, and how we evolve after reaching a big goal.

Beyond racing, Carson is building something much bigger: Dirt Camp Racing. JP and Carson dig into how the team was born, what makes it special, and how it balances supporting elite-level racers alongside beginners and youth riders. It’s a powerful conversation about mentorship, culture, and community-building in cycling. Whether you’re chasing podiums or simply riding for wellness, Carson’s story will leave you feeling inspired and grounded.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Selene Yeager | How to Show Up for the Women in Your Life | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Selene Yeager | How to Show Up for the Women in Your Life | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
Mental Health Project : https://www.indiegogo.com/projects/the-stable-cyclist-maah-daah-hey-film-3/x/33398252#/

ROAR: https://tinyurl.com/jw3kmn2b

NEXT LEVEL: https://tinyurl.com/yms3ue6c

In this week's episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, JP sits down with Selene Yeager—known to many as the FitChick from Bicycling magazine and more recently as a leading voice in women’s performance health. Selene shares her path from writing for Rodale Press to co-authoring game-changing books like ROAR and NEXT LEVEL with Dr. Stacy Sims. Now the host of the massively successful Hit Play Not Pause podcast on Feisty Media, Selene is leading the conversation around menopause, fitness, and what it means to thrive through midlife.

But this episode isn't just for women. JP and Selene dive into what men need to understand about the women around them—whether they’re partners, friends, athletes, or daughters. From hormonal shifts to mental health challenges, the episode explores how men can support women with empathy, awareness, and action. This is a must-listen for anyone who wants to better understand the women around them and be part of a stronger, more informed cycling and life community.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

Selene shares her path from writing for Rodale Press to co-authoring game-changing books like ROAR and NEXT LEVEL with Dr. Stacy Sims. Now the host of the massively successful Hit Play Not Pause podcast on Feisty Media, Selene is leading the conversation around menopause, fitness, and what it means to thrive through midlife.

But this episode isn't just for women. JP and Selene dive into what men need to understand about the women around them—whether they’re partners, friends, athletes, or daughters. From hormonal shifts to mental health challenges, the episode explores how men can support women with empathy, awareness, and action. This is a must-listen for anyone who wants to better understand the women around them and be part of a stronger, more informed cycling and life community.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: The Hotdish | A Race, A Rescue and One Wild Year | The Bootleg Miles
item.itunes:title: The Hotdish | A Race, A Rescue and One Wild Year | The Bootleg Miles
item.description:
Mental Health Project : https://www.indiegogo.com/projects/the-stable-cyclist-maah-daah-hey-film-3/x/33398252#/

Chase Wark Docu-Series : https://youtu.be/lxHdcdeQ7Bk?si=Rue4xkplPKh7psJQ

How much has your life changed in the last year? One year ago, I met Chase Wark and was struggling to find my rhythm with the podcast and with my projects. Fast forward a year and Chase is not only a friend but I work for him and he is my main sponsor for racing.

So after a wild few weeks of travel, I found myself in the Driftless region set to tackle The Hotdish, Chase's most Minnesotan race. I had great legs and was ready to roll but was faced with a decision just 6 miles into the race. This episode of the Bootleg Miles is as full as many swerves as the Hotdish course itself.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Crystal Kovacs | Redefining Gravel Culture with the Rise of Dirt Squad Cycling | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Crystal Kovacs | Redefining Gravel Culture with the Rise of Dirt Squad Cycling | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
Mental Health Project : https://www.indiegogo.com/projects/the-stable-cyclist-maah-daah-hey-film-3/x/33398252#/

This week on The Stable Cyclist Podcast, JP sits down with Crystal Kovacs—founder and driving force behind Dirt Squad Cycling—for a heartfelt and honest conversation recorded live at the 2025 Unbound Expo. Crystal opens up about her personal journey into gravel cycling, the barriers she encountered, and the moment she realized that the sport needed more intentional pathways for people who don’t fit the "traditional" cyclist mold.

We dive into how Dirt Squad was born not out of performance goals, but out of a deeper mission: building community and creating a radically inclusive space in gravel. Whether you're a longtime rider or just gravel-curious, Crystal’s philosophy of riding with heart—and riding together—is inspiring and energizing. If you love the soul and ethos of The Stable Cyclist, you’ll feel right at home with Dirt Squad.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Badlands Gravel Battle - Where One Conversation Changed My Life | The Bootleg Miles | Episode 7
item.itunes:title: Badlands Gravel Battle - Where One Conversation Changed My Life | The Bootleg Miles | Episode 7
item.description:
Mental Health Project : https://www.indiegogo.com/projects/the-stable-cyclist-maah-daah-hey-film-3/x/33398252#/

Chase Wark Docu-Series : https://youtu.be/lxHdcdeQ7Bk?si=Rue4xkplPKh7psJQ In this episode, I return to the Badlands Gravel Battle—the race that unknowingly changed the trajectory of my life back in 2018. That year, I arrived unknowingly battling Bipolar Disorder, and yet I found myself in a rare stretch of natural stability. I was fit, focused, and I won. But what came after the finish line would shape everything: Nick and Lindsay Ybarra encouraged me to take on the Maah Daah Hey 100. That one conversation became the spark that carried me through a storm I didn’t yet know was coming.

This vlog is about racing, yes—but it’s also about mental health, resilience, and the power of encouragement. As I race the 2025 edition of the Badlands Gravel Battle, I reflect on how that first race gave me a purpose when my life was falling apart. That memory became a lifeline through my diagnosis in 2020 and fueled the story I’m now telling through the film Back to the Start. Watch as I return to where it all began, chasing finish lines and healing in the North Dakota Badlands.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Bobby Wintle | Music, Mid-South, and the Emotional Toll of Gravel's Biggest Events | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Bobby Wintle | Music, Mid-South, and the Emotional Toll of Gravel's Biggest Events | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
Mental Health Project : https://igg.me/at/lKG7bQK7eJc/x/33398252#/

Chase Wark Docu-Series : https://youtu.be/lxHdcdeQ7Bk?si=rdpFwJj8MJeIPN6D

In this deeply personal and emotionally charged episode, recorded inside Merchant Cycles in Emporia, Kansas just before Unbound 2025, host John Peter sits down with Bobby Wintle—race director of the legendary Mid-South gravel race—for a raw and reflective conversation.

Bobby opens up about his life before gravel as a passionate musician, and how that creative energy helped shape the welcoming, inclusive spirit of Mid-South. Together, the two discuss the emotional highs and crushing lows of putting on massive events, including the heartbreak and mental spiral following the wildfire-forced cancellation of Mid-South earlier this year. This is an intimate exchange between two high-energy, heart-forward people—offering a rare look behind the curtain of what it means to love, build, and carry a community through gravel cycling.

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.itunes:summary: Mental Health Project : https://igg.me/at/lKG7bQK7eJc/x/33398252#/ Chase Wark Docu-Series : https://youtu.be/lxHdcdeQ7Bk?si=rdpFwJj8MJeIPN6D In this deeply personal and emotionally charged episode, recorded inside Merchant Cycles in Emporia, Kansas just before Unbound 2025, host John Peter sits down with Bobby Wintle—race director of the legendary Mid-South gravel race—for a raw and reflective conversation. Bobby opens up about his life before gravel as a passionate musician, and how that creative energy helped shape the welcoming, inclusive spirit of Mid-South. Together, the two discuss the emotional highs and crushing lows of putting on massive events, including the heartbreak and mental spiral following the wildfire-forced cancellation of Mid-South earlier this year. This is an intimate exchange between two high-energy, heart-forward people—offering a rare look behind the curtain of what it means to love, build, and carry a community through gravel cycling. item.title: Ian Boswell | Unbound, Team Sky and Redefining Cycling | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Ian Boswell | Unbound, Team Sky and Redefining Cycling | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
What happens when a pro cyclist walks away at the top? Ian Boswell joins us for a deep and honest conversation about his years with Team Sky and Katusha, the toll of concussions, and the identity crisis that followed his Unbound Gravel win. In this vulnerable episode, Ian opens up about leaving WorldTour racing, redefining success off the podium, and why he's now focused on the beauty happening behind the front of the race.

Ian shares how he's using his platform to help the sport grow. This conversation wraps up our Month of May and the Gravel Gospel series in the most powerful way possible.

Support JP's next big cycling/mental health project : https://igg.me/at/lKG7bQK7eJc/x/33398252#/

item.title: Logan Kasper on Unbound XL, FKTs, and the Truth About Winning | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Logan Kasper on Unbound XL, FKTs, and the Truth About Winning | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, ultra-endurance legend Logan Kasper reflects on the highs, lows, and honest truths of his racing career. From winning Unbound XL to setting the Eastern Divide FKT, Logan has carved out a reputation as one of gravel’s most driven and underrated riders.

But what happens after a win at one of the sport’s most grueling events? Logan shares why winning Unbound XL didn’t actually change his life, and how his runner-up finish the next year felt like the perfect closing chapter. We also dive into the mindset behind ultra-distance riding, what defines success, and what is the best gas station food.

Support JP's next big cycling/mental health project : https://igg.me/at/lKG7bQK7eJc/x/33398252#/

Read about Logan's Eastern Divide FKT: https://bikepacking.com/bikes/logan-kasper-eastern-divide-trail-record/

item.title: Getting the Lauf Seigla Ready to Ride | The Bootleg Miles | Episode 6
item.itunes:title: Getting the Lauf Seigla Ready to Ride | The Bootleg Miles | Episode 6
item.description:
Mental Health Project: https://tinyurl.com/me5h4zme

Maah Daah Hey: https://tinyurl.com/bddhjse4

Maah Daah Hey: TOGETHER: https://tinyurl.com/369c6an9

Silca Blog: https://tinyurl.com/265pa7pz

Bankruptcy Bike Build: https://tinyurl.com/tnak2svs

Silca Support for Mental Health Project: https://tinyurl.com/me5h4zme

Two weeks ago at Granny’s Gravel Grinder, I received an incredible surprise: a brand new Lauf Seigla thanks to Chase Wark and Wark’s Cycling Services coming on board as a sponsor. But here's the twist... it took me a full week to actually ride the thing! In this episode of Bootleg Miles, I walk you through the process of modding and customizing my new gravel race bike — and why dialing in a ride when you're 6'4" is no small feat. From chain waxing to bar swaps to internal brake routing, this was more than a quick tune-up.

This pod isn’t just about building bikes — it’s about why the process matters. I share how wrenching helps my mental health, how I taught myself bike mechanics out of necessity, and how I finally brought White Lightning 2.0 to life. We also talk Silca sealant, tubeless setups, and why this is the nicest bike I’ve ever owned. If you’re a gear nerd, love behind-the-scenes storytelling, or just want to see how this bike came together before Badlands Gravel Battle, you’re in the right place.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Amanda Nauman Sheek | A Hall of Fame Career Built on Support | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Amanda Nauman Sheek | A Hall of Fame Career Built on Support | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
Amanda Nauman Sheek is being inducted into the Gravel Cycling Hall of Fame—but her biggest wins aren’t just on paper. In this episode, Amanda reflects on what’s truly defined her career: not just Unbound and MidSouth victories, but the behind-the-scenes support, mentorship, and community that made those moments possible. As she prepares for her Hall of Fame speech, she shares how legacy, not just results, has become her focus.

We dig into what it takes to create lasting change in gravel cycling—especially for women and girls. Amanda talks candidly about what men can do to be better allies, how to support new rides, and why building inclusive spaces is key to the sport’s future. This conversation isn’t just about racing—it’s about the deeper work that makes success possible.

item.title: Thomas Euler | Storytelling is the Future of Gravel | The Stable Cyclist EMERGENCY Podcast
item.itunes:title: Thomas Euler | Storytelling is the Future of Gravel | The Stable Cyclist EMERGENCY Podcast
item.description:
In this emergency episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, we dive deep into the power of storytelling with Germany’s gravel media mastermind Thomas Euler. Known for his work on the Aut-Said Gravel and Gracing podcasts alongside Paul Voss, his writing on the Gravel Stack Substack, and published feature in Escape Collective, Thomas brings a unique media-savvy lens to the world of gravel cycling.

We explore why storytelling is the key to growing this beautiful sport, how his experience working in media strategy for NBA teams informs his approach, and what gravel can learn from other sports to build a sustainable future. Whether you're a racer, fan, or creative in the gravel scene, this conversation unpacks the systems, stories, and shifts that can take gravel to the next level.

item.title: Pete Stetina | Gravel Was Salvation to My Cycling Career | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Pete Stetina | Gravel Was Salvation to My Cycling Career | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
Pete Stetina is one of the most passionate figures in American cycling especially when it comes to the discipline of gravel. In this deeply personal episode of The Gravel Gospel, Pete opens up about the long road from WorldTour stardom to gravel salvation. We revisit his days riding with some of the biggest teams in the world, including helping Ryder Hesjedal win the 2012 Giro d’Italia and surviving a devastating leg injury while riding for BMC in 2015 at the Tour of the Basque Country. Pete takes us inside the physical and emotional toll of that crash, and how it nearly ended everything.

But gravel saved him. As a rider with Trek, Pete cautiously dipped a toe into the world of off-road racing, only to find something deeper than podiums: purpose, community, and freedom. This is a rare look at the heart behind the racer, and a raw conversation with someone who redefined what a post-WorldTour career can look like. Whether you’re new to gravel or a longtime fan of Pete, this interview will change how you see one of the sport’s most unique voices.

Support JP's next big cycling/mental health project : https://igg.me/at/lKG7bQK7eJc/x/33398252#/

item.title: Granny’s Gravel Grinder | My Wildest Gravel Adventure EVER!!! The Bootleg Miles | Episode 5
item.itunes:title: Granny’s Gravel Grinder | My Wildest Gravel Adventure EVER!!! The Bootleg Miles | Episode 5
item.description:
What happens when you show up to race... but miss the start because you're filming the race director for a documentary? That’s exactly what went down at Granny’s Gravel Grinder, a Central Minnesota gravel race hosted by Chase Wark and Wark's Cycling Services. With over 400 riders across multiple distances, the event brought big community energy, rowdy midwestern grit, and hospitality only Chase can deliver. For JP — back in the gravel game for the first time in two years — it meant lining up Dead F**ing Last (DFL)* after capturing pre-race footage for his upcoming docu-series on Chase.

Despite the late start, the day turned into something far more meaningful. The post-race celebration held a surprise for JP: a brand-new Lauf Siegla and a sponsorship offer from Wark Cycling Services to support his storytelling work on The Stable Cyclist. This video captures the ride, the vibes, and how JP's work has shaped Chase's life over the past year. Whether you're here for gravel racing, cycling documentaries, or the power of people supporting people — this one’s for you.

Support JP's next big cycling/mental health project : https://igg.me/at/lKG7bQK7eJc/x/33398252#/

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Ben Delaney | Gravel Evangelist & Veteran of Cycling Journalism | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Ben Delaney | Gravel Evangelist & Veteran of Cycling Journalism | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
In this special Gravel Gospel episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, JP welcomes one of gravel cycling’s most respected voices—Ben Delaney. With a journalism résumé that includes VeloNews, CyclingNews, BikeRadar, Outside, CyclingTips, Cycling Weekly, and Cyclist, Ben has shaped how we talk about bikes for nearly two decades. Now forging his own path on YouTube—free of corporate sponsorships—Ben gives his honest takes on bikes, gear, and the ever-evolving gravel scene.

Together, JP and Ben dive into what makes gravel so magnetic, from gas station stops to the community-first spirit that defines the sport. They also talk about content creation, staying true to your voice, and which gravel races seem extra special these days. Whether you're a gravel diehard or just gravel-curious, this episode will make you want to get dusty and ride.

Follow Ben on Instagram for more content: @ben_delaney Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: The Gravel Gospel is Here on The Stable Cyclist
item.itunes:title: The Gravel Gospel is Here on The Stable Cyclist
item.description: In the month of May, we will be taking everyone on a journey through the Gravel Gospel as we anticipate Unbound.  Instead of our normal 2-3 guests, JP sits down with gravel evangelists Ben Delaney, Amanda Nauman-Sheek, Pete Stetina, Logan Kasper and Ian Boswell.  And if we say our prayers correctly we may even be in for a massive surprise or two late in the month.  A LOT going on here right now so be sure you are subscribed and make sure those notifications are turned on and most importantly, remember, you are loved.

item.title: Building a Cycling and Mental Health Movement | The Bootleg Miles | Episode 4
item.itunes:title: Building a Cycling and Mental Health Movement | The Bootleg Miles | Episode 4
item.description:
HERE IS WHERE YOU CAN JOIN THE MOVEMENT: https://igg.me/at/lKG7bQK7eJc/x/33398252#/

In this episode of The Bootleg Miles, JP shares an exciting development: the next installment in the Maah Daah Hey film series, Back to the Start, is underway. This project delves deeper into the intertwining paths of cycling and mental health, offering a narrative that's both personal and universally resonant. As the journey unfolds, there's a unique opportunity for the community to play a pivotal role in bringing this story to life.

JP discusses how this film isn't just about the miles ridden but the stories shared, the challenges faced, and the collective strength found in unity. By engaging with this project, you're not only supporting a film but becoming part of a movement that seeks to inspire, connect, and foster understanding through the lens of cycling and personal growth.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

In this episode of The Bootleg Miles, JP shares an exciting development: the next installment in the Maah Daah Hey film series, Back to the Start, is underway. This project delves deeper into the intertwining paths of cycling and mental health, offering a narrative that's both personal and universally resonant. As the journey unfolds, there's a unique opportunity for the community to play a pivotal role in bringing this story to life.

JP discusses how this film isn't just about the miles ridden but the stories shared, the challenges faced, and the collective strength found in unity. By engaging with this project, you're not only supporting a film but becoming part of a movement that seeks to inspire, connect, and foster understanding through the lens of cycling and personal growth.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Colleen Maher: From Couch Potato to Gravel National Champion
item.itunes:title: Colleen Maher: From Couch Potato to Gravel National Champion
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, I sit down with Colleen Maher, whose rise in gravel racing is nothing short of incredible. Just three years ago, she was a self-proclaimed couch potato—now, she’s a national champion. We discuss her rapid ascension in the sport, what it’s like to experience so much success so quickly, and how that can lead to an unexpected identity crisis. When cycling becomes such a big part of who you are, what happens next?

Beyond racing, we dive into the struggle of making new friends as an adult and how cycling has helped her build a strong community. Colleen also shares insights on getting more beginners involved in the sport and why progress looks different for everyone. Whether you’re just getting into cycling or have been riding for years, this episode is packed with inspiration and real talk about the challenges of personal growth in sport and life.

Follow Colleen on Instagram for more content: @colleenmaher007

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com
