item.title: My Real Bonk Bros Story | The Bootleg Miles | Episode 3
item.itunes:title: My Real Bonk Bros Story | The Bootleg Miles | Episode 3
item.description:
In this episode of The Bootleg Miles, I’m finally telling the full story of my love for the Bonk Bros. But this is more than just fanboy appreciation. This is a story about the kindness of strangers, unsolicited support and finding your way back after everything falls apart.

In the midst of a massive mental health project in 2023, I had a devastating mountain bike crash and head injury sidelining me for over a year, I wasn’t sure if I’d ever ride again. But the guys in the Bonk Bros helped in the ways they could, and Adam Saban stuck with me. Now we’re working together to prepare for the Maah Daah Hey 100 and to finish that massive Mental Health Project—and this episode is a tribute to the people who never stopped believing. If you’ve ever struggled to get back up, or wondered if your story still matters, I hope this video reminds you that you are not alone. Subscribe for more stories at the intersection of cycling, community, and mental health.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: The One Choice That Changed My Life | The Bootleg Miles | Episode 2
item.itunes:title: The One Choice That Changed My Life | The Bootleg Miles | Episode 2
item.description:
In this episode of Bootleg Miles, JP opens up about something far harder than finishing a grueling 100-mile mountain bike race: asking for help with his mental health. As a teacher, filmmaker, and endurance athlete living with Bipolar Disorder, JP reflects on the fears that held him back from getting help — and the life-changing shift that happened when he finally did. Filmed on the bike and from the heart, this ride dives deep into the raw, often unspoken challenges of living with mental illness.

Whether you're struggling yourself or supporting someone who is, this episode tackles real mental health conversations head-on — including how coaching, therapy, and community can make all the difference. If you're navigating anxiety, depression, or Bipolar Disorder, this story might be the nudge you need to ask for help. And if you're just here for a ride, trust us — this one goes way deeper than watts.

This series is brought to you by Wahoo (www.wahoofitness.com)

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Kelly Jones | Overcoming Addiction with Rediscovery on the Bike | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Kelly Jones | Overcoming Addiction with Rediscovery on the Bike | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
Kelly Jones is a two-time national champion in eSports and eMTB, but her path to the top was anything but typical. In this powerful episode, she shares her journey from growing up in England to moving to the U.S. at 15, and how a snowboarding accident changed the course of her life. For the first time, Kelly talks about how that injury led to a long struggle with addiction and abuse—but it was the bike that ultimately became her lifeline.

After rehab, Kelly found a new identity and purpose through cycling. We talk about the highs and lows of recovery, the role of mental health, and how racing gave her a way forward. This conversation is a raw and inspiring look at transformation, resilience, and the power of sport to rebuild a life.

Follow Kelly on Instagram for more content: kellyjonesmtb

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Why Community Matters More Than Ever | The Bootleg Miles | Episode 1
item.itunes:title: Why Community Matters More Than Ever | The Bootleg Miles | Episode 1
item.description:
Welcome to the first episode of The Bootleg Miles. Where we'll all come along on the journey this year as I look to finish the 3rd film in the Maah Daah Hey Film Series.

This week, we'll start by looking back at the premiere tour for Maah Daah Hey: Together and the moment a room full of strangers became something much more.

From Knoxville, TN to Fargo, ND, we sat down and shared stories—about bikes, yes—but also about friendship. And in a time when we seem divided on everything, that shared space meant everything.

This series is brought to you by Wahoo. (www.wahoofitness.com)

🎬 *Back to the Start*, the next MDH film, begins production this summer.

🟡 Want to support the film? I'll share that information next week in our next episode.

🔔 Subscribe for the ride: races, recovery, and real conversations.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

item.title: UPDATE: A New Stable Cyclist Mini-Series is Here
item.itunes:title: UPDATE: A New Stable Cyclist Mini-Series is Here
item.description:
Running through the spring and the summer, The Stable Cyclist presents a new mini-series within The Stable Cyclist's feed: The Bootleg Miles.  This new series will focus on JP and his stories as he prepares to film the 3rd film in the Maah Daah Hey Series, a mental health film about his own journey.  This won't be a BTS style show though.  JP will take you to races, you'll meet his coach and sometimes he'll really open up about his mental health.  Wahoo Fitness (www.https://www.wahoofitness.com/) has come on board to help sponsor this series and the work JP is doing!  

Episodes of the Bootleg Miles will typically drop on Mondays or Wednesdays and the first full episode will drop tomorrow.  

Also, DON'T WORRY!  The regular long form interviews will keep appearing on Fridays and I have some really big name guests coming your way in the next 6 weeks.  

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

The companies supporting JP and this channel:

Maplelag Resort - https://www.maplelag.com/

Flow Formulas - https://flowformulas.com/discount/JOHNPETER15

Owl City Cycles - https://www.owlcitycycles.com

The Black Bibs - https://www.theblackbibs.com

Ignition Coach Co. - https://www.ignitioncoachco.com/

Mental Health Advocacy - Go4Graham - https://www.go4graham.org/

Resources: 988 Lifeline: https://988lifeline.org/

International Bipolar Foundation: https://ibpf.org/

National Association for Mental Illness: https://www.nami.org/

item.title: James Huang | N-1, Honest Reviews, and the Wild Ride at CyclingTips | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: James Huang | N-1, Honest Reviews, and the Wild Ride at CyclingTips | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, I sit down with James Huang, one of the most respected voices in cycling tech. James now runs N-1, his Substack dedicated to honest, unbiased bike reviews, but his career has taken him through some of the biggest names in cycling media. We discuss his journey, the importance of integrity in product reviews, and how advertising dollars can sometimes muddy the waters of journalism.

But the heart of this conversation is James’ time at CyclingTips, where he was part of a team that felt more like family—until Outside bought the company and tore it apart. James stayed until the bitter end, not out of loyalty to the new management, but to provide for his family. We talk about what it’s like when a job you once loved turns into something you can’t stand, and how to navigate the emotional and financial strain of staying in a toxic work environment. If you’ve ever felt stuck in a job you can’t leave, this episode will hit home.

Read James' work: nminus1bikes.substack.com

Follow James on Instagram for more content: @angryasian

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

The companies supporting JP and this channel:

Maplelag Resort - https://www.maplelag.com/

Flow Formulas - https://flowformulas.com/discount/JOHNPETER15

Owl City Cycles - https://www.owlcitycycles.com

The Black Bibs - https://www.theblackbibs.com

Ignition Coach Co. - https://www.ignitioncoachco.com/

Mental Health Advocacy - Go4Graham - https://www.go4graham.org/

Resources: 988 Lifeline: https://988lifeline.org/

International Bipolar Foundation: https://ibpf.org/

National Association for Mental Illness: https://www.nami.org/

item.title: Hannah and Kevin Simms | Insta Contest Winners to Cape Epic Contenders | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Hannah and Kevin Simms | Insta Contest Winners to Cape Epic Contenders | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
Six weeks. That’s all the time Hannah Simms and her father, Kevin Simms, had to prepare for one of the toughest mountain bike stage races in the world—the Cape Epic. When Hannah unexpectedly won an entry through an Instagram contest, the father-daughter duo jumped at the chance to take on the grueling multi-day race in South Africa. Despite the short notice, they not only finished but placed 5th overall in the Mixed GC battle, proving that sometimes the best adventures are the ones you never planned.

In this episode, we dive into the chaos of their last-minute entry, the highs and lows of racing Cape Epic as a team, and the unique experience of tackling an endurance event as a father-daughter duo. Hannah and Kevin share what it took to prepare, how they supported each other through brutal stages, and why this race became more than just a competition—it was an unforgettable bonding experience. Whether you're a fan of MTB racing, adventure stories, or just love seeing family push limits together, this is an episode you won’t want to miss!

Follow Hannah on Instagram for more content: @hannahsimms11

Follow Kevin on Instagram for more content: @coachkevinsimms

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

The companies supporting JP and this channel: Maplelag Resort - https://www.maplelag.com/

Flow Formulas - https://flowformulas.com/discount/JOHNPETER15

Owl City Cycles - https://www.owlcitycycles.com

The Black Bibs - https://www.theblackbibs.com

Ignition Coach Co. - https://www.ignitioncoachco.com/

Mental Health Advocacy - Go4Graham - https://www.go4graham.org/

Resources: 988 Lifeline: https://988lifeline.org/

International Bipolar Foundation: https://ibpf.org/

National Association for Mental Illness: https://www.nami.org/

item.title: Abi Snyder | Overcoming Public Fear | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Abi Snyder | Overcoming Public Fear | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, I sit down with professional enduro and marathon XC racer Abigail Snyder, who I first met at the Maah Daah Hey: Together premiere in Roanoke. Abigail shares her journey in the sport and her work to lower the barriers of entry for women in mountain biking. But beyond her racing career, she opens up about a moment that made her unexpectedly go viral—a video of her having a panic attack mid-jump. Meant to show that even pros struggle, the video led to an onslaught of online attacks, mostly from men, forcing her to confront the darker side of vulnerability in the public eye.

We dive deep into what it means to be open about struggles while navigating the toxicity that can come with it. Abigail shares how therapy helped her regain confidence on the bike and why creating space for mental health conversations in cycling is so important. This is a powerful and necessary discussion about resilience, online criticism, and the mental side of being an athlete. Whether you’re a cyclist, an advocate for women in sport, or someone who has faced public scrutiny, this episode is one you won’t want to miss.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

Follow Abi on Instagram for more content: @abi.ridefierce

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Ask JP Anything | Ignoring Drama, Film Premieres, and Much More | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Ask JP Anything | Ignoring Drama, Film Premieres, and Much More | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
This episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast is all about you—our listeners! In our first ASK JP ANYTHING episode of 2025, I tackle your questions about everything from best bar tape to podcasting to filmmaking to life itself. Why didn’t I dig into the break-up drama of previous guests when we talk about their relationships ending? Why do we have in-person premieres after a film is already on YouTube? And how did I become good at listening to others? We cover all that and much more in this candid, no-holds-barred episode.

Beyond answering your questions, I dive into the philosophy behind my approach—why I choose to focus on meaningful conversations over controversy, the importance of community-driven storytelling, and how listening can be one of the most powerful tools in life. Whether you’re here for cycling insights, filmmaking strategies, or just a behind-the-scenes look at the podcast, this episode has something for everyone. Tune in for an open and honest discussion shaped entirely by your questions!

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

The companies supporting JP and this channel:

Owl City Cycles - https://www.owlcitycycles.com

The Black Bibs - https://www.theblackbibs.com

Ignition Coach Co. - https://www.ignitioncoachco.com/

Mental Health Advocacy - Go4Graham - https://www.go4graham.org/

item.title: Ted King and Chase Wark | MidSouth, Wildfires, and Giving Back | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Ted King and Chase Wark | MidSouth, Wildfires, and Giving Back | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
In this special collaborative episode between King of the Ride Podcast and The Stable Cyclist Podcast, I sit down with Ted King and Chase Wark to talk about their unforgettable weekend at MidSouth. Just as the event was about to kick off, devastating wildfires swept through Stillwater, OK, destroying homes and forcing the race’s cancellation. But instead of heading home, both Ted and Chase—without speaking to each other beforehand—felt compelled to do something bigger. They set out on the Mega-Mid-South route, a grueling 300-mile gravel loop, to raise awareness and funds for those affected by the fires.

The cycling world followed along in real-time as Ted set off nearly an hour ahead of Chase, both attempting the Fastest Known Time (FKT) on the route. In a dramatic finish, Ted set the record before Chase arrived just minutes later, besting Ted’s time. Now, both riders are using their platforms to continue raising money for Stillwater and its surrounding communities. Tune in to hear their firsthand account of an extraordinary weekend, the power of the cycling community, and how we can all support those in need.

If you are looking to give towards either rider's efforts, you can Venmo them below and let them know it's for Payne County United Way.

Ted King: @iamtedking

Chase Wark: @chase-wark

Please continue to look out for those around you and remember you are loved.

Thumbnail photo credit: Tilly Shull

You can find more of Tilly's work on Insta: @thistilly

#thestablecyclist #tedking #chasewark #midsouth
item.content:encoded:
In this special collaborative episode between King of the Ride Podcast and The Stable Cyclist Podcast, I sit down with Ted King and Chase Wark to talk about their unforgettable weekend at MidSouth. Just as the event was about to kick off, devastating wildfires swept through Stillwater, OK, destroying homes and forcing the race’s cancellation. But instead of heading home, both Ted and Chase—without speaking to each other beforehand—felt compelled to do something bigger. They set out on the Mega-Mid-South route, a grueling 300-mile gravel loop, to raise awareness and funds for those affected by the fires.

The cycling world followed along in real-time as Ted set off nearly an hour ahead of Chase, both attempting the Fastest Known Time (FKT) on the route. In a dramatic finish, Ted set the record before Chase arrived just minutes later, besting Ted’s time. Now, both riders are using their platforms to continue raising money for Stillwater and its surrounding communities. Tune in to hear their firsthand account of an extraordinary weekend, the power of the cycling community, and how we can all support those in need.

If you are looking to give towards either rider's efforts, you can Venmo them below and let them know it's for Payne County United Way.

Ted King: @iamtedking

Chase Wark: @chase-wark

Please continue to look out for those around you and remember you are loved.

Thumbnail photo credit: Tilly Shull

You can find more of Tilly's work on Insta: @thistilly

#thestablecyclist #tedking #chasewark #midsouth

item.title: Kerry Werner | Rediscovering Identity after Job Loss and Divorce | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Kerry Werner | Rediscovering Identity after Job Loss and Divorce | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, I sit down with Kerry Werner to talk about the arc of his career from XCO MTB to Cyclocross to his transition to racing gravel. We also talk about major life changes that have reshaped his identity. Kerry opens up about what it was like to lose his job as a pro cyclist while going through a divorce in 2023—two massive shifts that forced him to redefine himself both on and off the bike. His honesty about navigating these challenges makes this one of the most raw and meaningful conversations we’ve had on the show.

Beyond the struggles, Kerry shares how he’s rebuilding his life and finding new purpose in content creation at his job with Bike Flights. We talk about resilience, personal growth, and how to move forward after life throws you unexpected setbacks. Whether you've faced job loss, a major life transition, or simply want to hear an inspiring story of reinvention, this episode is a must-listen.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

Follow Kerry on Instagram for more content: @kerryw24

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Nina Machina | Building a Cycling Career Beyond the Influencer Spotlight
item.itunes:title: Nina Machina | Building a Cycling Career Beyond the Influencer Spotlight
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, I sit down with Nina Machina, pro bike racer and social media influencer, to discuss her remarkable journey. Nina shares her experience rising to social media stardom in 8th grade and how the sudden attention shaped her identity and mental health. We explore her efforts to transition into the pro cycling world and the challenge of being recognized as a serious athlete rather than just an influencer.

Nina also talks about her passion for giving back to the sport through her Mach 1 U23 Development Team, which she started to help young cyclists access elite racing opportunities. This conversation is a powerful exploration of resilience, authenticity, and paying it forward. Whether you’re an athlete, a creator, or someone inspired by stories of overcoming challenges, this episode offers deep insights and motivation.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

Follow Nina on Instagram for more content: @nina.machina

Follow Mach 1 Performance for more content: @mach1__performance

And also: www.mach1-performance.com

item.title: Scott McGill | The Stable Cyclist's White Whale | EMERGENCY Podcast
item.itunes:title: Scott McGill | The Stable Cyclist's White Whale | EMERGENCY Podcast
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, I finally sit down with the white whale—Scott McGill. Having had all the other Bonk Bros hosts on the show, it was only a matter of time before I tracked Scott down for an interview and landing him warranted an EMERGENCY POD.

A professional road racer and one of the original voices of Bonk Bros, Scott shares his journey to the pro ranks and how his perspective on life extends far beyond the bike. We dive deep into the evolution of his identity, discussing how he balances being an athlete, coach, and person outside of sport.

Scott also breaks down his coaching philosophy and how his experiences have shaped his approach to training and mentorship. He emphasizes the importance of mindset, balance, and looking at the bigger picture—both for himself and his athletes. Whether you're a cycling fan, an athlete, or someone trying to navigate identity beyond a career or passion, this episode delivers insight, humor, and a long-awaited conversation that was well worth the chase.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

Follow Scott on Instagram for more content: @scottmcgilljr

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Andrew Vontz | How Sleep is the Ultimate Performance Hack | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Andrew Vontz | How Sleep is the Ultimate Performance Hack | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, I sit down with Andrew Vontz—former Strava VP, writer for the LA Times, and co-founder of The Better Lab, a cutting-edge sleep app. Andrew shares insights from his fascinating career, from his pivotal role at TRX during its rise to success to his time at Strava and beyond. We discuss how listening, observing, and learning from others have been crucial in every stage of his work. Andrew’s thoughtful reflections on his career offer valuable lessons for anyone striving to grow personally and professionally.

We also take a deep dive into his latest project, The Better Lab, exploring the critical role sleep plays in mental and physical well-being. Because sleep and mental health are core to maintaining balance as an athlete and in life, Andrew breaks down how gaining control of your sleep can have a transformative impact. Whether you're a cyclist looking to optimize recovery or someone seeking to improve your daily life, this conversation is full of practical insights and inspiration.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

Follow Andrew on Instagram for more content: @hardwaypod

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

item.title: Samantha Bosco’s Journey | From Tragedy to Paralympic Gold | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.itunes:title: Samantha Bosco’s Journey | From Tragedy to Paralympic Gold | The Stable Cyclist Podcast
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, we sit down with Samantha Bosco, a Paralympic gold medalist whose incredible journey embodies resilience, determination, and the power of community. Samantha shares her story of discovering her passion for cycling, which propelled her to the Rio Paralympics, and the unexpected challenges that followed. Just before the Tokyo Olympic Games, a devastating bike accident left her with a traumatic brain injury, jeopardizing her career and shaking her sense of identity. Samantha candidly discusses the physical and emotional toll of this period and the unwavering determination it took to get back on the bike.

Through grit, perseverance, and the unwavering support of those around her, Samantha defied the odds and returned stronger than ever, ultimately winning gold at the Paris Olympics. Tune in as we explore the mental and physical struggles she faced, her strategies for overcoming adversity, and her message about the importance of surrounding yourself with a supportive community. Whether you're a cyclist, an athlete, or simply someone looking for inspiration, Samantha’s story will leave you motivated and ready to tackle any challenge.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist Follow Samantha Bosco on Instagram for more content: @sammiecranks

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com

The companies supporting JP and this channel: Maplelag Resort - https://www.maplelag.com/ Flow Formulas - https://flowformulas.com/discount/JOHNPETER15 Owl City Cycles - www.owlcitycycles.com 701 Cycle and Sport - www.701cycleandsport.com Ignition Coach Co. - https://www.ignitioncoachco.com/ Mental Health Advocacy - Go4Graham - https://www.go4graham.org/ Resources: 988 Lifeline: https://988lifeline.org/ International Bipolar Foundation: https://ibpf.org/ National Association for Mental Illness: https://www.nami.org/

Tune in as we explore the mental and physical struggles she faced, her strategies for overcoming adversity, and her message about the importance of surrounding yourself with a supportive community. Whether you're a cyclist, an athlete, or simply someone looking for inspiration, Samantha’s story will leave you motivated and ready to tackle any challenge. item.title: Dylan Johnson | The Mental Fight Behind His Best Year Ever
item.itunes:title: Dylan Johnson | The Mental Fight Behind His Best Year Ever
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist podcast, pro cyclist and YouTube sensation Dylan Johnson opens up about his journey through personal and physical challenges. Known for his analytical take on cycling performance and aero gains, Dylan shares a rarely-seen side of himself, discussing the depression he faced after a breakup and how he used cycling as a tool for healing. He dives into the struggles of managing recurring throat abscesses and the resilience it took to overcome them. Despite these obstacles, Dylan reflects on how he turned adversity into triumph, achieving his best year yet on the bike.

This conversation goes beyond watts and wind tunnels, offering an inspiring look at the mental and emotional side of elite cycling. Dylan's candid discussion reminds us all of the power of perseverance, self-reflection, and finding purpose through hardship. Whether you're a fan of his content or someone looking for motivation to tackle life's challenges, this episode is not to be missed.

The Stable Cyclist podcast is an every-other week podcast where JP and his guests dive into bikes, life mentality and when the opportunity presents itself, they will dive deep into mental health.

You can drop a note to JP over at thestablecyclist@gmail.com and every so often, he'll answer them on an Ask JP Anything episode.

item.title: Elliott Waldock | Cocadona, Tahoe and the Maah Daah Hey Trail
item.itunes:title: Elliott Waldock | Cocadona, Tahoe and the Maah Daah Hey Trail
item.description:
In this episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, we sit down with trail running extraordinaire Elliott Waldock to explore his incredible journey through the ultra-running world. From tackling the grueling 250 miles of Cocadona to navigating the Tahoe 200, Elliott shares stories of perseverance, adventure, and the mental benefits of endurance exercise. As the course record holder for both the Maah Daah Hey 150 and Maah Daah Hey 100, Elliott takes us deep into the beauty and challenge of North Dakota's iconic trail. Whether you're a runner, cyclist, or lover of the outdoors, this episode is packed with inspiration and practical insights for anyone chasing big goals on the trails.

You can find Elliott on instagram @elliott___w

Watch his record setting race in the MDH150: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=J6xW-e47wgI&t=18s

Watch his Cocadona 250 race: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5b8_zZQXy9Y

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com.

In one week, the newest chapter in the Maah Daah Hey film series drops when MAAH DAAH HEY: TOGETHER releases worldwide on YouTube at 7:00 pm Central Time.

#Cocadona #northdakotalegendary #ultrarunning

item.title: Gordon Wadsworth and Thomas Turner | Friendship and Adventure
item.itunes:title: Gordon Wadsworth and Thomas Turner | Friendship and Adventure
item.description:
This week on the Stable Cyclist Podcast, JP is joined or rather JP joins Gordon Wadsworth and Thomas "the Hitman" Turner in Tennessee. Whether racing the Maah Daah Hey 100 or smashing through the Pisgah forests, Gordon Wadsworth and Thomas Turner are up for any adventure you can come up with. The pair are the focus of the upcoming documentary Maah Daah Hey: Together. This film is the next chapter in the Maah Daah Hey film series and will be available to the public January 10th, and 7pm (central).

 

The Trailer can be seen here: https://youtu.be/usKyRk4XY74?si=7gYuCGa050TESJPB

item.title: We Are BACK!!!!!
item.itunes:title: We Are BACK!!!!!
item.description: After three months away taking care of other parts of our lives, Aaron and JP return to talk about what it was like to film this year's Maah Daah Hey 100.  JP reveals the MDH Film Series he is working on as well as dropping some big news regarding his racing in the coming year.  And then there is so much more!  The podcast will return to an every other week format in the coming weeks with a fun interview with Gordon Wadsworth and Thomas Turner coming up in two weeks.  Thanks for listening and remember you are loved!

item.title: Claire O'Leary | Winning the DAMn Race and Empowering Others in Cycling
item.itunes:title: Claire O'Leary | Winning the DAMn Race and Empowering Others in Cycling
item.description:
Join us on The Stable Cyclist Podcast featuring ultra-endurance cyclist Claire O'Leary from Minnesota. Claire, who finished 3rd in the open field at Unbound 2023 and recently won the Day Across Minnesota (DAMn), shares her incredible journey into the world of endurance cycling. In this episode, Claire opens up about her past struggles, including overcoming an eating disorder, and how she transitioned from running to becoming a successful and competitive cyclist.

Claire also discusses her passion for helping more not-men get involved in the sport, offering valuable insights and advice for those looking to take on the challenges of endurance cycling. Tune in to hear Claire's powerful story of resilience, determination, and her commitment to growing the cycling community. Available now on YouTube, Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and your favorite podcast platforms.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com.

item.title: Shaums March and Anthony Taylor on Black Bike Week and Diversifying Cycling
item.itunes:title: Shaums March and Anthony Taylor on Black Bike Week and Diversifying Cycling
item.description:
JP is joined by Anthony Taylor, founder of Black Bike Week in Minneapolis, and former Red Bull Professional MTB rider Shaums March. Together, they discuss the significance of Black Bike Week in promoting diversity and inclusion in a sport that has traditionally been less accessible to communities of color. Anthony and Shaums emphasize the importance of introducing cycling to everyone, creating opportunities for all to experience the joy and benefits of riding.

Shaums, who is also the president of the Bike Instructor Certification Program, shares his insights on the critical need for teaching kids basic bike skills that can level the playing field on the trails, even against those with greater fitness. The conversation also delves into Shaums’ work with elite riders like Kate Courtney and Haley Batten on the US National Team, highlighting the impact of skill development at the highest levels of the sport. Tune in for an inspiring discussion on the future of cycling, diversity, and the power of education.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com.

item.title: Nathan Stoa | When You Can't Control the Controllable
item.itunes:title: Nathan Stoa | When You Can't Control the Controllable
item.description:
In a joint podcast with the Eyes Up Mindset podcast, one of JP's good friends, Hawley, MN Head Basketball Coach Nathan Stoa joins the program this week to have a serious discussion about building programs in youth sports and finding what actually matters as a teacher and coach.

Eyes Up host Jamie Wagner and JP then dive deep into the fallout from a family situation in Coach Stoa's life and how he was able to navigate the mental health crisis that arrived in the wake of the family situation. Despite no mention of bikes on this episode, it is a very real encounter with family trauma and mental health and once again, why community is so important.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com.

Eyes Up host Jamie Wagner and JP then dive deep into the fallout from a family situation in Coach Stoa's life and how he was able to navigate the mental health crisis that arrived in the wake of the family situation. Despite no mention of bikes on this episode, it is a very real encounter with family trauma and mental health and once again, why community is so important. Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com. item.title: Check-In with Chase Wark: Getting Unstuck
item.itunes:title: Check-In with Chase Wark: Getting Unstuck
item.description:
Welcome back to The Stable Cyclist Podcast with returning guest Chase Wark! In this episode, Chase shares the highlights and challenges of his past six weeks, including some impressive race results and the disappointment of the Crusher in the Tushar being canceled. Chase opens up about feeling stuck during our last conversation and reveals how he managed to get unstuck.

We also dive into the much-anticipated update on the "gravel beef" between Chase and Drew Dillman, bringing some entertaining and candid moments to the discussion. Tune in for an episode filled with race recaps, personal growth, and a bit of friendly rivalry.

Follow Chase on Instagram for more content: @chasethebiker

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

item.title: Drew Dillman | Faith, Community and Gravel Beef
item.itunes:title: Drew Dillman | Faith, Community and Gravel Beef
item.description:
Welcome back to The Stable Cyclist Podcast with returning guest Drew Dillman! After being prank called by Chase Wark and JP in a previous episode, Drew is here to set the record straight on their entertaining "gravel-beef." In this deep and insightful conversation, Drew opens up about his personal identity and shares his faith in Jesus, providing a profound look into what drives him.

Drew also discusses the importance of community and explains why he chooses to stay in his small community in Kentucky. Additionally, we explore Ignition Coach Co., the coaching company founded by Drew and Dylan Johnson, and its mission to support and develop cyclists. Tune in for an episode filled with humor, heartfelt stories, and valuable insights on YouTube, Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and your favorite podcast platforms.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com.

item.title: Ask JP Episode 2: Concussions, Bipolar Mania and Crying during Wrestlemania?!?
item.itunes:title: Ask JP Episode 2: Concussions, Bipolar Mania and Crying during Wrestlemania?!?
item.description:
Welcome to the second Ask JP Anything episode on The Stable Cyclist Podcast. Aaron Haugen and Jamie Wagner join to co-host this one as the trio dive into where JP is at in his post-concussion journey, how JP's experiences with Mania and Depression have differed, why it's important to have difficult conversations and finally the boys press JP if it is true that he cried watching this year's main event of Wrestlemania.

If you’d like to ask JP a question, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com. Or if you are listening on Spotify, you can ask a question in the Q&A section of the episode. Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

item.title: Paul Wichmann | A 17 Year Run Streak and Working for Tyler the Creator
item.itunes:title: Paul Wichmann | A 17 Year Run Streak and Working for Tyler the Creator
item.description:
In this powerful episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast, JP sits down with his best friend from high school, Paul Wichmann, who is currently the front of house soundman for Tyler the Creator. Paul shares fascinating insights into his job with Tyler, offering a behind-the-scenes look at the music industry. But this episode goes much deeper, as Paul opens up about the difficult period he faced in college when his parents suddenly divorced, leading to a struggle with depression.

We also discuss Paul's incredible commitment to running and his 17 year run streak. His story is a testament to resilience and finding ways to stay motivated and mentally healthy. Tune in to hear this inspiring conversation about overcoming life's challenges and getting unstuck from depression. Available now on YouTube, Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and your favorite podcast platforms. 

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

For more information about Lyons-Heart Gravel Classic: https://www.bikesignup.com/Race/ND/Mandan/LyonsHeartGravelClassic

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com.

item.title: Tyler Cloutier's Cycling Career: The Working Man's Inspiration
item.itunes:title: Tyler Cloutier's Cycling Career: The Working Man's Inspiration
item.description:
Join us for an inspiring episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast featuring former professional cyclocross racer Tyler Cloutier. Tyler achieved the true working man's dream by balancing a full-time job while racing professionally across the United States and Europe. In this episode, host JP delves into Tyler's journey, exploring the challenges and triumphs of his dual career. Now a coach and a vocal advocate for everyone to get on bikes, Tyler shares his passion for cycling and his insights on making the sport accessible to all.

We also discuss Tyler's current role as a member of the Bonk Bros podcast, where he continues to influence the cycling community. This conversation offers a unique glimpse into the life of a dedicated athlete who has successfully merged his professional and racing careers. Tune in to The Stable Cyclist Podcast on YouTube, Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and your favorite podcast platforms to hear Tyler's story and his vision for the future of cycling.

Follow Tyler on Instagram for more content: @tylerclouti

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

For more information about Lyons-Heart Gravel Classic: https://www.bikesignup.com/Race/ND/Mandan/LyonsHeartGravelClassic

item.title: Chase Wark | Extreme Honesty About Life as a Pro
item.itunes:title: Chase Wark | Extreme Honesty About Life as a Pro
item.description:
Join us for an exhilarating episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast featuring professional gravel racer Chase Wark. In this in-person interview at Chase's house, we dive deep into his experience with the Lifetime Grand Prix and his incredible bike setup for the Unbound Gravel race. We also delve into the entertaining "Gravel Beef" with Drew Dillman, even making a cold call to Drew to discuss it firsthand. Chase opens up about the significant risks he took by leaving a full-time job to pursue a career as a professional racer, shedding light on the challenges and loneliness that come with the professional lifestyle.

Listen as Chase shares his inspiring journey from being dropped 5 miles into the juniors race at nationals to becoming a full-time professional cyclist. He discusses the relentless grind and dedication required to reach the top. This episode is filled with candid moments and insights that will resonate with any cycling enthusiast. Tune in now on YouTube, Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and your favorite podcast platforms to catch this must-listen conversation.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com.

item.itunes:summary: Join us for an exhilarating episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast featuring professional gravel racer Chase Wark. In this in-person interview at Chase's house, we dive deep into his experience with the Lifetime Grand Prix and his incredible bike setup for the Unbound Gravel race. We also delve into the entertaining "Gravel Beef" with Drew Dillman, even making a cold call to Drew to discuss it firsthand. Chase opens up about the significant risks he took by leaving a full-time job to pursue a career as a professional racer, shedding light on the challenges and loneliness that come with the professional lifestyle. Listen as Chase shares his inspiring journey from being dropped 5 miles into the juniors race at nationals to becoming a full-time professional cyclist. He discusses the relentless grind and dedication required to reach the top. This episode is filled with candid moments and insights that will resonate with any cycling enthusiast. Tune in now on YouTube, Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and your favorite podcast platforms to catch this must-listen conversation. Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com. item.title: Bruce Martens | Empowering Youth Through Cycling and Community
item.itunes:title: Bruce Martens | Empowering Youth Through Cycling and Community
item.description:
Join us for an engaging episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast featuring Bruce Martens, founder of the National Center for Youth Development in Minneapolis, MN. Bruce, an elite-level racer, shares his passion for inclusively getting kids on bikes and the critical role well-trained adults play in supporting these young cyclists. Discover how the organization is making a difference in the lives of youth through cycling, promoting physical activity, and fostering a sense of community.

In this episode, we explore why the bike is such a powerful tool for mental health management and the positive impact it has on children's lives. Bruce discusses his three core values of Community, Identity, and Purpose, and how they shape the mission of his organization. With a mix of insightful discussions and some light-hearted shenanigans, this interview is both informative and entertaining. Tune in now on YouTube, Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and your favorite podcast platforms to hear Bruce's inspiring story and his vision for youth development through cycling.

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com.

item.title: Joshua Strong | Cycling Photography, Cyclocross Triumphs, and LGBTQ+ Advocacy
item.itunes:title: Joshua Strong | Cycling Photography, Cyclocross Triumphs, and LGBTQ+ Advocacy
item.description:
Join us for an enlightening episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast featuring elite cycling photographer Joshua Strong. Joshua, who is BIPOC and identifies as transgender, shares their journey and achievements in the world of cycling photography and their recent success in the non-binary division at cyclocross nationals. We also dive into the mental health crisis affecting the LGBTQ+ community and the importance of having conversations with people from diverse backgrounds to foster understanding and create a better world. 

 

Follow Joshua on Instagram for more content: @_joshuawstrong 

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

 

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com.

item.title: Kelly Catale Part 2 | Overcoming Injury and Mental Health Challenges in MTB
item.itunes:title: Kelly Catale Part 2 | Overcoming Injury and Mental Health Challenges in MTB
item.description:
Welcome back to The Stable Cyclist Podcast for part two of our captivating interview with Kelly Catale, the reigning U.S. Singlespeed MTB Champion! In this episode, Kelly shares the inspiring story behind the birth of the KellCat Devo Team and the challenges of securing new sponsors each year. We delve into the mental health struggles and realities Kelly faced while battling post-concussion syndrome after two accidents in 2023, and how she found renewed joy and a new identity through her journey. Don't miss this compelling conversation that highlights resilience, determination, and the power of passion. Tune in now on YouTube, Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and your favorite podcast platforms.

Follow Kelly on Instagram for more content: @kelly.catale

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

If you’d like to ask JP a question for an upcoming Ask JP Anything episode, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com.

item.title: Kelly Catale Part 1 | Mountain Biking, Mental Health and Daily Authenticity
item.itunes:title: Kelly Catale Part 1 | Mountain Biking, Mental Health and Daily Authenticity
item.description:
Join us for another episode of The Stable Cyclist Podcast featuring National Champion Kelly Catale! In this first part of our two-part series, Kelly shares her incredible journey transitioning from running to mountain biking, highlighting the unparalleled freedom she found on two wheels. Dive deep into Kelly's passion for mental health advocacy as we explore her "Mental Health Mondays Check-In" on Instagram—a powerful initiative promoting mental well-being. Kelly's insights on the importance of daily commitment and authenticity will leave you motivated and empowered. Don't miss this engaging conversation available now on YouTube, Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and all your favorite podcast platforms.

Follow Kelly on Instagram for more content: @kelly.catale

item.title: Ask JP Anything #1 - Did You Really Celebrate on a 15 year old? Living With Bipolar and More
item.itunes:title: Ask JP Anything #1 - Did You Really Celebrate on a 15 year old? Living With Bipolar and More
item.description:
Welcome to the first Ask JP Anything episode on The Stable Cyclist Podcast. Aaron Haugen joins as well to co-host this one as the pair dive into if JP actually celebrated on a 15 year old kid in a MTB race, how to find the right therapist, why JP transitioned from running to the bike, his relationship with his family when he struggles with depression and how did he talk with his kids about his diagnosis.  

If you’d like to ask JP a question, send him a DM on instagram @thestablecyclist or send an email to thestablecyclist@gmail.com.  

 

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

item.title: Dr. Sam Rachwarter | The Endurance Physio: Overcoming Adversity and Embracing Life
item.itunes:title: Dr. Sam Rachwarter | The Endurance Physio: Overcoming Adversity and Embracing Life
item.description:
Delve into the extraordinary journey of Dr. Sam Rauchwarter, renowned as the Endurance Physio, in this compelling episode hosted by JP. As one of the top endurance physical therapists in the United States, Dr. Rauchwarter brings a wealth of expertise and insight into his work with professional athletes, navigating the complexities of physical performance and rehabilitation. Despite grappling with hemophilia, Dr. Rauchwarter has forged a path of athletic achievement, defying limitations and inspiring others with his resilience and determination.

In a deeply personal and poignant conversation, JP and Dr. Rauchwarter explore the profound experience of grief and the challenge of moving forward when mental health becomes stagnant after the loss of a loved one. Drawing from his own journey of loss and healing, Dr. Rauchwarter shares invaluable perspectives and strategies for navigating the grieving process, offering hope and guidance to listeners who may be facing similar struggles. Tune in to gain profound insights into resilience, healing, and the transformative power of embracing life's challenges.

Listen to Sam’s podcast: https://tinyurl.com/35c8da84

Follow Sam on Instagram for more content: @theendurancephysio

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist 

 

The companies supporting JP and this channel

item.title: Dr. Beth Peter | Exercise and Mental Health Discussions
item.itunes:title: Dr. Beth Peter | Exercise and Mental Health Discussions
item.description:
Join host John Peter and his special guest, Dr. Beth Peter from Holland, Michigan, as they delve into the transformative power of nature on mental health and strategies for approaching conversations about mental wellness with loved ones. In this enlightening episode, Dr. Peter, sister to the host, shares insights drawn from both professional expertise and personal experience. Together, they explore the profound benefits of outdoor activities on mental well-being, offering practical advice for incorporating nature into daily life.

With warmth and candor, John and Dr. Peter navigate the delicate topic of discussing mental health with loved ones, offering empathetic guidance and communication strategies that promote understanding and support. Whether you're seeking ways to enhance your own mental wellness or looking for effective ways to support someone you care about, this episode provides invaluable insights and practical tools for fostering meaningful connections and holistic well-being. Tune in to gain a deeper understanding of the intersection between nature, mental health, and compassionate communication.

Step it Up Events - Ottawa County, Michigan (miottawa.org) https://www.miottawa.org/Parks/stepitup-events.htm

Park Passport - Outdoor Discovery Center https://outdoordiscovery.org/park-passport/

Why Our Brains Need the Outdoors | Psychology Today https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/blog/healthy-brain-happy-life/202107/why-our-brains-need-the-outdoors#:~:text=Although%20the%20studies%20are%20mostly%20preliminary%2C%20with%20small,disorders%2Faddictions%2C%20depression%2C%20anxiety%2C%20post-traumatic%20stress%20disorder%2C%20and%20insomnia.

Nature-based outdoor activities for mental and physical health: Systematic review and meta-analysis - PMC (nih.gov) https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8498096/

Residential green space in childhood is associated with lower risk of psychiatric disorders from adolescence into adulthood | PNAS https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.1807504116

Green Space and Stress: Evidence from Cortisol Measures in Deprived Urban Communities - PMC (nih.gov) https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3799530/

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

International Bipolar Foundation: https://ibpf.org/

National Association for Mental Illness: https://www.nami.org/

item.title: Nick Ybarra | Saving America's Longest Contiguous Singletrack Trail
item.itunes:title: Nick Ybarra | Saving America's Longest Contiguous Singletrack Trail
item.description:
Nick Ybarra is the founder of the Maah Daah Hey 100 and the Badlands Races Series. He is also the Director of the Save the MDH non-profit organization that annually keeps the entire Maah Daah Hey trail system alive and functional for it's users. But beyond that, Nick is continually looking for ways to improve, enhance and protect the trail, whether that's with local trail advocacy or working with Congress in Washington D.C.

Today Nick comes on the podcast to chat with JP about the work they are doing as well as some wild tales from the trails and of course Nick and JP can never get together without diving into the beloved Maah Daah Hey 100.

Watch the MAAH DAAH HEY Film here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UjEGq2S9vcs&t=2678s

Follow Nick on Instagram for more content: @maahdaahhey100

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

item.title: Tinker Juarez | American MTB Legend!
item.itunes:title: Tinker Juarez | American MTB Legend!
item.description:
This week on the Stable Cyclist, we journey through the world of BMX and mountain biking as we sit down with none other than the legendary Tinker Juarez. We dive deep into Tinker's illustrious career, from his humble beginnings to becoming a trailblazer in the mountain biking world. We chat about his 24 hour world championships battle with Kelly Magelky and we park for a while at the Maah Daah Hey 100. Finally we dive into his break-up with Cannondale and his rocky relationship with Floyd Landis.

 

Watch the MAAH DAAH HEY Film here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UjEGq2S9vcs&t=2678s

Follow Tinker on Instagram for more content: @tinker.juarez

Follow JP on Instagram for more content: @thestablecyclist

item.title: Charlotte Backus-Turner | Life Beyond Winning
item.itunes:title: Charlotte Backus-Turner | Life Beyond Winning
item.description:
Charlotte Backus-Turner is a cyclist and coach out of Park City, Utah. She mixes her background as a UCI Road Cyclist and Zwift Academy racer with her education in Psychology and well as extensive study in nutrition as she works with athletes in her company (www.exquisiteendurancecoaching.com). Today she and JP discuss nutritional errors athletes are making as well as how to operate at your healthiest as an athlete.

Nutrition Partner - Flow Formulas - https://flowformulas.com/discount/JOHNPETER15

item.title: Adam Saban | Not Just a Bonk Bro.
item.itunes:title: Adam Saban | Not Just a Bonk Bro.
item.description:
Adam Saban is most famously known as the host of the Bonk Bros Podcast and the Matchbox Podcast, but he's also a coach, elite level mtb rider, an engineer, a husband, a dad and so much more. In the first real interview Adam has done outside the Bonk Bros, we get to know him and realize, he's not just a Bonk Bro.  We dive into how Adam became a cyclist, why Leadville is so special to him, how he became the Bonk Bros host, and we dive into how mental health fits within his story.   

 

Nutrition Partner - Flow Formulas - https://flowformulas.com/discount/JOHNPETER15

You can follow Adam on Instagram @adamsaban6

You can follow JP on Instagram @thestablecyclist

item.content:encoded:
Adam Saban is most famously known as the host of the Bonk Bros Podcast and the Matchbox Podcast, but he's also a coach, elite level mtb rider, an engineer, a husband, a dad and so much more. In the first real interview Adam has done outside the Bonk Bros, we get to know him and realize, he's not just a Bonk Bro.  We dive into how Adam became a cyclist, why Leadville is so special to him, how he became the Bonk Bros host, and we dive into how mental health fits within his story.   

 

Nutrition Partner - Flow Formulas - https://flowformulas.com/discount/JOHNPETER15

You can follow Adam on Instagram @adamsaban6

You can follow JP on Instagram @thestablecyclist

item.title: Dylan Johnson | An Unapologetic Try-Hard
item.itunes:title: Dylan Johnson | An Unapologetic Try-Hard
item.description:
Dylan Johnson is participating in the Lifetime Grand Prix for the 3rd year in a row.  He is one of the top Gravel and MTB professionals in the United States.  He is also one of the most popular YouTubers in the bike coaching genre.  On top of all that, he is co-owner of Ignition Coach Co. a coaching company.  

 

Dylan and I dive into the world of YouTube, what it was like to take on giants in the cycling industry like Zwift and TrainerRoad, and we dive into his obsessive personality.  

 

You can follow Dylan on Instagram @dylanjawnson

You can follow JP on Instagram @thestablecyclist

item.title: Andrew L ’Esperance | Managing Stressful Situations
item.itunes:title: Andrew L ’Esperance | Managing Stressful Situations
item.description:
Andrew L 'Esperance is a professional mountain bike and gravel racer who is entering his third season in the Lifetime Grand Prix (LTGP). Prior to the Grand Prix, he made a living as a World Cup MTB racer around the world. But he is perhaps equally famous for being the partner of Haley Smith, winner of the 2022 LTGP. Haley has been one of the most outspoken athletes in the bike world with regards to mental health. I wanted to chat with Andrew about his development as an athlete but also, I wanted to hear what it is like to be the #1 support person in a relationship where mental health is a regular discussion.
Follow Andrews's story: https://www.instagram.com/andrewlespy
Follow JP's story: https://www.instagram.com/thestablecyclist The companies supporting me and this channel Maplelag Resort - https://www.maplelag.com/ Nutrition Partner - Flow Formulas - https://flowformulas.com/discount/JOHNPETER15 Bike Shop Partner - https://www.701cycleandsport.com/ Marginal Gains Partner - Silca - https://silca.cc/ Coaching - Adam Saban and Ignition Coach Co. - https://www.ignitioncoachco.com/ Mental Health Advocacy - Go4Graham - https://www.go4graham.org/ Resources: 988 Lifeline: https://988lifeline.org/ International Bipolar Foundation: https://ibpf.org/ National Association for Mental Illness: https://www.nami.org/
